verses.kjv: Where are they? where are thy wise men? and let them tell thee now, and let them know what the Lord of hosts hath purposed upon Egypt.
verses.bsb: Where are your wise men now? Let them tell you and reveal what the LORD of Hosts has planned against Egypt.

verses.esv: The princes of Zoan have become fools, and the princes of Memphis are deluded; those who are the cornerstones of her tribes have made Egypt stagger.
verses.kjv: The princes of Zoan are become fools, the princes of Noph are deceived; they have also seduced Egypt, even they that are the stay of the tribes thereof.
verses.bsb: The princes of Zoan have become fools; the princes of Memphis are deceived. The cornerstones of her tribes have led Egypt astray.

verses.esv: The LORD has mingled within her a spirit of confusion, and they will make Egypt stagger in all its deeds, as a drunken man staggers in his vomit.
verses.kjv: The Lord hath mingled a perverse spirit in the midst thereof: and they have caused Egypt to err in every work thereof, as a drunken man staggereth in his vomit.
verses.bsb: The LORD has poured into her a spirit of confusion. Egypt has been led astray in all she does, as a drunkard staggers through his own vomit.

verses.esv: And there will be nothing for Egypt that head or tail, palm branch or reed, may do.
verses.kjv: Neither shall there be any work for Egypt, which the head or tail, branch or rush, may do.
verses.bsb: There is nothing Egypt can do— head or tail, palm or reed.

verses.esv: In that day the Egyptians will be like women, and tremble with fear before the hand that the LORD of hosts shakes over them.
verses.kjv: In that day shall Egypt be like unto women: and it shall be afraid and fear because of the shaking of the hand of the Lord of hosts, which he shaketh over it.
verses.bsb: In that day the Egyptians will be like women. They will tremble with fear beneath the uplifted hand of the LORD of Hosts, when He brandishes it against them.

verses.esv: And the land of Judah will become a terror to the Egyptians. Everyone to whom it is mentioned will fear because of the purpose that the LORD of hosts has purposed against them.
verses.kjv: And the land of Judah shall be a terror unto Egypt, every one that maketh mention thereof shall be afraid in himself, because of the counsel of the Lord of hosts, which he hath determined against it.
verses.bsb: The land of Judah will bring terror to Egypt; whenever Judah is mentioned, Egypt will tremble over what the LORD of Hosts has planned against it.

verses.esv: In that day there will be five cities in the land of Egypt that speak the language of Canaan and swear allegiance to the LORD of hosts. One of these will be called the City of Destruction.
verses.kjv: ¶ In that day shall five cities in the land of Egypt speak the language of Canaan, and swear to the Lord of hosts; one shall be called, The city of destruction.
verses.bsb: In that day five cities in the land of Egypt will speak the language of Canaan and swear allegiance to the LORD of Hosts. One of them will be called the City of the Sun.

verses.esv: In that day there will be an altar to the LORD in the midst of the land of Egypt, and a pillar to the LORD at its border.
verses.kjv: In that day shall there be an altar to the Lord in the midst of the land of Egypt, and a pillar at the border thereof to the Lord.
verses.bsb: In that day there will be an altar to the LORD in the center of the land of Egypt, and a pillar to the LORD near her border.

verses.esv: It will be a sign and a witness to the LORD of hosts in the land of Egypt. When they cry to the LORD because of oppressors, he will send them a savior and defender, and deliver them.
verses.kjv: And it shall be for a sign and for a witness unto the Lord of hosts in the land of Egypt: for they shall cry unto the Lord because of the oppressors, and he shall send them a saviour, and a great one, and he shall deliver them.
verses.bsb: It will be a sign and a witness to the LORD of Hosts in the land of Egypt. When they cry out to the LORD because of their oppressors, He will send them a savior and defender to rescue them.

verses.esv: And the LORD will make himself known to the Egyptians, and the Egyptians will know the LORD in that day and worship with sacrifice and offering, and they will make vows to the LORD and perform them.
verses.kjv: And the Lord shall be known to Egypt, and the Egyptians shall know the Lord in that day, and shall do sacrifice and oblation; yea, they shall vow a vow unto the Lord, and perform it.
verses.bsb: The LORD will make Himself known to Egypt, and on that day Egypt will acknowledge the LORD. They will worship with sacrifices and offerings; they will make vows to the LORD and fulfill them.

verses.esv: And the LORD will strike Egypt, striking and healing, and they will return to the LORD, and he will listen to their pleas for mercy and heal them.
verses.kjv: And the Lord shall smite Egypt: he shall smite and heal it: and they shall return even to the Lord, and he shall be intreated of them, and shall heal them.
verses.bsb: And the LORD will strike Egypt with a plague; He will strike them but heal them. They will turn to the LORD, and He will hear their prayers and heal them.

verses.esv: In that day there will be a highway from Egypt to Assyria, and Assyria will come into Egypt, and Egypt into Assyria, and the Egyptians will worship with the Assyrians.
verses.kjv: ¶ In that day shall there be a highway out of Egypt to Assyria, and the Assyrian shall come into Egypt, and the Egyptian into Assyria, and the Egyptians shall serve with the Assyrians.
verses.bsb: In that day there will be a highway from Egypt to Assyria. The Assyrians will go to Egypt, and the Egyptians to Assyria. The Egyptians and Assyrians will worship together.

verses.esv: In that day Israel will be the third with Egypt and Assyria, a blessing in the midst of the earth,
verses.kjv: In that day shall Israel be the third with Egypt and with Assyria, even a blessing in the midst of the land:
verses.bsb: In that day Israel will join a three-party alliance with Egypt and Assyria—a blessing upon the earth.

verses.esv: whom the LORD of hosts has blessed, saying, “Blessed be Egypt my people, and Assyria the work of my hands, and Israel my inheritance.”
verses.kjv: Whom the Lord of hosts shall bless, saying, Blessed be Egypt my people, and Assyria the work of my hands, and Israel mine inheritance.
verses.bsb: The LORD of Hosts will bless them, saying, “Blessed be Egypt My people, Assyria My handiwork, and Israel My inheritance.”

verses.esv: In the year that the commander in chief, who was sent by Sargon the king of Assyria, came to Ashdod and fought against it and captured it—
verses.kjv: IN the year that Tartan came unto Ashdod, (when Sargon the king of Assyria sent him,) and fought against Ashdod, and took it;
verses.bsb: Before the year that the chief commander, sent by Sargon king of Assyria, came to Ashdod and attacked and captured it,

verses.esv: at that time the LORD spoke by Isaiah the son of Amoz, saying, “Go, and loose the sackcloth from your waist and take off your sandals from your feet,” and he did so, walking naked and barefoot.
verses.kjv: At the same time spake the Lord by Isaiah the son of Amoz, saying, Go and loose the sackcloth from off thy loins, and put off thy shoe from thy foot. And he did so, walking naked and barefoot.
verses.bsb: the LORD had already spoken through Isaiah son of Amoz, saying, “Go, remove the sackcloth from your waist and the sandals from your feet.”  And Isaiah did so, walking around naked and barefoot.

verses.esv: Then the LORD said, “As my servant Isaiah has walked naked and barefoot for three years as a sign and a portent against Egypt and Cush,
verses.kjv: And the Lord said, Like as my servant Isaiah hath walked naked and barefoot three years for a sign and wonder upon Egypt and upon Ethiopia;
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said, “Just as My servant Isaiah has gone naked and barefoot for three years as a sign and omen against Egypt and Cush,

verses.esv: so shall the king of Assyria lead away the Egyptian captives and the Cushite exiles, both the young and the old, naked and barefoot, with buttocks uncovered, the nakedness of Egypt.
verses.kjv: So shall the king of Assyria lead away the Egyptians prisoners, and the Ethiopians captives, young and old, naked and barefoot, even with their buttocks uncovered, to the shame of Egypt.
verses.bsb: so the king of Assyria will lead away the captives of Egypt and the exiles of Cush, young and old alike, naked and barefoot, with bared buttocks—to Egypt’s shame.

verses.esv: Then they shall be dismayed and ashamed because of Cush their hope and of Egypt their boast.
verses.kjv: And they shall be afraid and ashamed of Ethiopia their expectation, and of Egypt their glory.
verses.bsb: Those who made Cush their hope and Egypt their boast will be dismayed and ashamed.

verses.esv: And the inhabitants of this coastland will say in that day, ‘Behold, this is what has happened to those in whom we hoped and to whom we fled for help to be delivered from the king of Assyria! And we, how shall we escape?’”
verses.kjv: And the inhabitant of this isle shall say in that day, Behold, such is our expectation, whither we flee for help to be delivered from the king of Assyria: and how shall we escape?
verses.bsb: And on that day the dwellers of this coastland will say, ‘See what has happened to our source of hope, those to whom we fled for help and deliverance from the king of Assyria! How then can we escape?’”

verses.esv: The oracle concerning the wilderness of the sea. As whirlwinds in the Negeb sweep on, it comes from the wilderness, from a terrible land.
verses.kjv: THE burden of the desert of the sea. As whirlwinds in the south pass through; so it cometh from the desert, from a terrible land.
verses.bsb: This is the burden against the Desert by the Sea:  Like whirlwinds sweeping through the Negev, an invader comes from the desert, from a land of terror.

verses.esv: A stern vision is told to me; the traitor betrays, and the destroyer destroys. Go up, O Elam; lay siege, O Media; all the sighing she has caused I bring to an end.
verses.kjv: A grievous vision is declared unto me; the treacherous dealer dealeth treacherously, and the spoiler spoileth. Go up, O Elam: besiege, O Media; all the sighing thereof have I made to cease.
verses.bsb: A dire vision is declared to me: “The traitor still betrays, and the destroyer still destroys. Go up, O Elam! Lay siege, O Media! I will put an end to all her groaning.”

verses.esv: Therefore my loins are filled with anguish; pangs have seized me, like the pangs of a woman in labor; I am bowed down so that I cannot hear; I am dismayed so that I cannot see.
verses.kjv: Therefore are my loins filled with pain: pangs have taken hold upon me, as the pangs of a woman that travaileth: I was bowed down at the hearing of it; I was dismayed at the seeing of it.
verses.bsb: Therefore my body is filled with anguish. Pain grips me, like the pains of a woman in labor. I am bewildered to hear, I am dismayed to see.

verses.esv: My heart staggers; horror has appalled me; the twilight I longed for has been turned for me into trembling.
verses.kjv: My heart panted, fearfulness affrighted me: the night of my pleasure hath he turned into fear unto me.
verses.bsb: My heart falters; fear makes me tremble. The twilight of my desire has turned to horror.

verses.esv: They prepare the table, they spread the rugs, they eat, they drink. Arise, O princes; oil the shield!
verses.kjv: Prepare the table, watch in the watchtower, eat, drink: arise, ye princes, and anoint the shield.
verses.bsb: They prepare a table, they lay out a carpet, they eat, they drink!   Rise up, O princes, oil the shields!

verses.esv: For thus the Lord said to me: “Go, set a watchman; let him announce what he sees.
verses.kjv: For thus hath the Lord said unto me, Go, set a watchman, let him declare what he seeth.
verses.bsb: For this is what the Lord says to me:   “Go, post a lookout and have him report what he sees.

verses.esv: When he sees riders, horsemen in pairs, riders on donkeys, riders on camels, let him listen diligently, very diligently.”
verses.kjv: And he saw a chariot with a couple of horsemen, a chariot of asses, and a chariot of camels; and he hearkened diligently with much heed:
verses.bsb: When he sees chariots with teams of horsemen, riders on donkeys, riders on camels, he must be alert, fully alert.”

verses.esv: Then he who saw cried out: “Upon a watchtower I stand, O Lord, continually by day, and at my post I am stationed whole nights.
verses.kjv: And he cried, A lion: My lord, I stand continually upon the watchtower in the daytime, and I am set in my ward whole nights:
verses.bsb: Then the lookout shouted:   “Day after day, my lord, I stand on the watchtower; night after night I stay at my post.

verses.esv: And behold, here come riders, horsemen in pairs!” And he answered, “Fallen, fallen is Babylon; and all the carved images of her gods he has shattered to the ground.”
verses.kjv: And, behold, here cometh a chariot of men, with a couple of horsemen. And he answered and said, Babylon is fallen, is fallen; and all the graven images of her gods he hath broken unto the ground.
verses.bsb: Look, here come the riders, horsemen in pairs.”   And one answered, saying:   “Fallen, fallen is Babylon! All the images of her gods lie shattered on the ground!”

verses.esv: O my threshed and winnowed one, what I have heard from the LORD of hosts, the God of Israel, I announce to you.
verses.kjv: O my threshing, and the corn of my floor: that which I have heard of the Lord of hosts, the God of Israel, have I declared unto you.
verses.bsb: O my people, crushed on the threshing floor, I tell you what I have heard from the LORD of Hosts, the God of Israel.

verses.esv: The oracle concerning Dumah. One is calling to me from Seir, “Watchman, what time of the night? Watchman, what time of the night?”
verses.kjv: ¶ The burden of Dumah. He calleth to me out of Seir, Watchman, what of the night? Watchman, what of the night?
verses.bsb: This is the burden against Dumah:  One calls to me from Seir, “Watchman, what is left of the night? Watchman, what is left of the night?”

verses.esv: The watchman says: “Morning comes, and also the night. If you will inquire, inquire; come back again.”
verses.kjv: The watchman said, The morning cometh, and also the night: if ye will inquire, inquire ye: return, come.
verses.bsb: The watchman replies, “Morning has come, but also the night. If you would inquire, then inquire. Come back yet again.”

verses.esv: The oracle concerning Arabia. In the thickets in Arabia you will lodge, O caravans of Dedanites.
verses.kjv: ¶ The burden upon Arabia. In the forest in Arabia shall ye lodge, O ye travelling companies of Dedanim.
verses.bsb: This is the burden against Arabia:  In the thickets of Arabia you must lodge, O caravans of Dedanites.

verses.esv: To the thirsty bring water; meet the fugitive with bread, O inhabitants of the land of Tema.
verses.kjv: The inhabitants of the land of Tema brought water to him that was thirsty, they prevented with their bread him that fled.
verses.bsb: Bring water for the thirsty, O dwellers of Tema; meet the refugees with food.

verses.esv: For they have fled from the swords, from the drawn sword, from the bent bow, and from the press of battle.
verses.kjv: For they fled from the swords, from the drawn sword, and from the bent bow, and from the grievousness of war.
verses.bsb: For they flee from the sword— the sword that is drawn— from the bow that is bent, and from the stress of battle.

verses.esv: For thus the Lord said to me, “Within a year, according to the years of a hired worker, all the glory of Kedar will come to an end.
verses.kjv: For thus hath the Lord said unto me, Within a year, according to the years of an hireling, and all the glory of Kedar shall fail:
verses.bsb: For this is what the Lord says to me: “Within one year, as a hired worker would count it, all the glory of Kedar will be gone.

verses.esv: And the remainder of the archers of the mighty men of the sons of Kedar will be few, for the LORD, the God of Israel, has spoken.”
verses.kjv: And the residue of the number of archers, the mighty men of the children of Kedar, shall be diminished: for the Lord God of Israel hath spoken it.
verses.bsb: The remaining archers, the warriors of Kedar, will be few.”  For the LORD, the God of Israel, has spoken.

verses.esv: The oracle concerning the valley of vision. What do you mean that you have gone up, all of you, to the housetops,
verses.kjv: THE burden of the valley of vision. What aileth thee now, that thou art wholly gone up to the housetops?
verses.bsb: This is the burden against the Valley of Vision:  What ails you now, that you have all gone up to the rooftops,

verses.esv: you who are full of shoutings, tumultuous city, exultant town? Your slain are not slain with the sword or dead in battle.
verses.kjv: Thou that art full of stirs, a tumultuous city, a joyous city: thy slain men are not slain with the sword, nor dead in battle.
verses.bsb: O city of commotion, O town of revelry?   Your slain did not die by the sword, nor were they killed in battle.

verses.esv: All your leaders have fled together; without the bow they were captured. All of you who were found were captured, though they had fled far away.
verses.kjv: All thy rulers are fled together, they are bound by the archers: all that are found in thee are bound together, which have fled from far.
verses.bsb: All your rulers have fled together, captured without a bow. All your fugitives were captured together, having fled to a distant place.

verses.esv: Therefore I said: “Look away from me; let me weep bitter tears; do not labor to comfort me concerning the destruction of the daughter of my people.”
verses.kjv: Therefore said I, Look away from me; I will weep bitterly, labour not to comfort me, because of the spoiling of the daughter of my people.
verses.bsb: Therefore I said, “Turn away from me, let me weep bitterly! Do not try to console me over the destruction of the daughter of my people.”

verses.esv: For the Lord GOD of hosts has a day of tumult and trampling and confusion in the valley of vision, a battering down of walls and a shouting to the mountains.
verses.kjv: For it is a day of trouble, and of treading down, and of perplexity by the Lord God of hosts in the valley of vision, breaking down the walls, and of crying to the mountains.
verses.bsb: For the Lord GOD of Hosts has set a day of tumult and trampling and confusion in the Valley of Vision— of breaking down the walls and crying to the mountains.

verses.esv: And Elam bore the quiver with chariots and horsemen, and Kir uncovered the shield.
verses.kjv: And Elam bare the quiver with chariots of men and horsemen, and Kir uncovered the shield.
verses.bsb: Elam takes up a quiver, with chariots and horsemen, and Kir uncovers the shield.

verses.esv: Your choicest valleys were full of chariots, and the horsemen took their stand at the gates.
verses.kjv: And it shall come to pass, that thy choicest valleys shall be full of chariots, and the horsemen shall set themselves in array at the gate.
verses.bsb: Your choicest valleys are full of chariots, and horsemen are posted at the gates.

verses.esv: He has taken away the covering of Judah. In that day you looked to the weapons of the House of the Forest,
verses.kjv: ¶ And he discovered the covering of Judah, and thou didst look in that day to the armour of the house of the forest.
verses.bsb: He has uncovered the defenses of Judah.   On that day you looked to the weapons in the House of the Forest.

verses.esv: and you saw that the breaches of the city of David were many. You collected the waters of the lower pool,
verses.kjv: Ye have seen also the breaches of the city of David, that they are many: and ye gathered together the waters of the lower pool.
verses.bsb: You saw that there were many breaches in the walls of the City of David. You collected water from the lower pool.

verses.esv: and you counted the houses of Jerusalem, and you broke down the houses to fortify the wall.
verses.kjv: And ye have numbered the houses of Jerusalem, and the houses have ye broken down to fortify the wall.
verses.bsb: You counted the houses of Jerusalem and tore them down to strengthen the wall.

verses.esv: You made a reservoir between the two walls for the water of the old pool. But you did not look to him who did it, or see him who planned it long ago.
verses.kjv: Ye made also a ditch between the two walls for the water of the old pool: but ye have not looked unto the maker thereof, neither had respect unto him that fashioned it long ago.
verses.bsb: You built a reservoir between the walls for the waters of the ancient pool, but you did not look to the One who made it, or consider Him who planned it long ago.

verses.esv: In that day the Lord GOD of hosts called for weeping and mourning, for baldness and wearing sackcloth;
verses.kjv: And in that day did the Lord God of hosts call to weeping, and to mourning, and to baldness, and to girding with sackcloth:
verses.bsb: On that day the Lord GOD of Hosts called for weeping and wailing, for shaven heads and the wearing of sackcloth.

verses.esv: and behold, joy and gladness, killing oxen and slaughtering sheep, eating flesh and drinking wine. “Let us eat and drink, for tomorrow we die.”
verses.kjv: And behold joy and gladness, slaying oxen, and killing sheep, eating flesh, and drinking wine: let us eat and drink; for to morrow we shall die.
verses.bsb: But look, there is joy and gladness, butchering of cattle and slaughtering of sheep, eating of meat and drinking of wine: “Let us eat and drink, for tomorrow we die!”

verses.esv: The LORD of hosts has revealed himself in my ears: “Surely this iniquity will not be atoned for you until you die,” says the Lord GOD of hosts.
verses.kjv: And it was revealed in mine ears by the Lord of hosts, Surely this iniquity shall not be purged from you till ye die, saith the Lord God of hosts.
verses.bsb: The LORD of Hosts has revealed in my hearing:  “Until your dying day, this sin of yours will never be atoned for,” says the Lord GOD of Hosts.

verses.esv: Thus says the Lord GOD of hosts, “Come, go to this steward, to Shebna, who is over the household, and say to him:
verses.kjv: ¶ Thus saith the Lord God of hosts, Go, get thee unto this treasurer, even unto Shebna, which is over the house, and say,
verses.bsb: This is what the Lord GOD of Hosts says: “Go, say to Shebna, the steward in charge of the palace:

verses.esv: What have you to do here, and whom have you here, that you have cut out here a tomb for yourself, you who cut out a tomb on the height and carve a dwelling for yourself in the rock?
verses.kjv: What hast thou here? and whom hast thou here, that thou hast hewed thee out a sepulchre here, as he that heweth him out a sepulchre on high, and that graveth an habitation for himself in a rock?
verses.bsb: What are you doing here, and who authorized you to carve out a tomb for yourself here—to chisel your tomb in the height and cut your resting place in the rock?

verses.esv: Behold, the LORD will hurl you away violently, O you strong man. He will seize firm hold on you
verses.kjv: Behold, the Lord will carry thee away with a mighty captivity, and will surely cover thee.
verses.bsb: Look, O mighty man! The LORD is about to shake you violently. He will take hold of you,

verses.esv: and whirl you around and around, and throw you like a ball into a wide land. There you shall die, and there shall be your glorious chariots, you shame of your master's house.
verses.kjv: He will surely violently turn and toss thee like a ball into a large country: there shalt thou die, and there the chariots of thy glory shall be the shame of thy lord’s house.
verses.bsb: roll you into a ball, and sling you into a wide land. There you will die, and there your glorious chariots will remain—a disgrace to the house of your master.

verses.esv: I will thrust you from your office, and you will be pulled down from your station.
verses.kjv: And I will drive thee from thy station, and from thy state shall he pull thee down.
verses.bsb: I will remove you from office, and you will be ousted from your position.

verses.esv: In that day I will call my servant Eliakim the son of Hilkiah,
verses.kjv: ¶ And it shall come to pass in that day, that I will call my servant Eliakim the son of Hilkiah:
verses.bsb: On that day I will summon My servant, Eliakim son of Hilkiah.

verses.esv: and I will clothe him with your robe, and will bind your sash on him, and will commit your authority to his hand. And he shall be a father to the inhabitants of Jerusalem and to the house of Judah.
verses.kjv: And I will clothe him with thy robe, and strengthen him with thy girdle, and I will commit thy government into his hand: and he shall be a father to the inhabitants of Jerusalem, and to the house of Judah.
verses.bsb: I will clothe him with your robe and tie your sash around him. I will put your authority in his hand, and he will be a father to the dwellers of Jerusalem and to the house of Judah.

verses.esv: And I will place on his shoulder the key of the house of David. He shall open, and none shall shut; and he shall shut, and none shall open.
verses.kjv: And the key of the house of David will I lay upon his shoulder; so he shall open, and none shall shut; and he shall shut, and none shall open.
verses.bsb: I will place on his shoulder the key to the house of David. What he opens no one can shut, and what he shuts no one can open.

verses.esv: And I will fasten him like a peg in a secure place, and he will become a throne of honor to his father's house.
verses.kjv: And I will fasten him as a nail in a sure place; and he shall be for a glorious throne to his father’s house.
verses.bsb: I will drive him like a peg into a firm place, and he will be a throne of glory for the house of his father.

verses.esv: And they will hang on him the whole honor of his father's house, the offspring and issue, every small vessel, from the cups to all the flagons.
verses.kjv: And they shall hang upon him all the glory of his father’s house, the offspring and the issue, all vessels of small quantity, from the vessels of cups, even to all the vessels of flagons.
verses.bsb: So they will hang on him all the glory of his father’s house: the descendants and the offshoots—all the lesser vessels, from bowls to every kind of jar.

verses.esv: In that day, declares the LORD of hosts, the peg that was fastened in a secure place will give way, and it will be cut down and fall, and the load that was on it will be cut off, for the LORD has spoken.”
verses.kjv: In that day, saith the Lord of hosts, shall the nail that is fastened in the sure place be removed, and be cut down, and fall; and the burden that was upon it shall be cut off: for the Lord hath spoken it.
verses.bsb: In that day, declares the LORD of Hosts, the peg driven into a firm place will give way; it will be sheared off and fall, and the load upon it will be cut down.”  Indeed, the LORD has spoken.

verses.esv: The oracle concerning Tyre. Wail, O ships of Tarshish, for Tyre is laid waste, without house or harbor! From the land of Cyprus it is revealed to them.
verses.kjv: THE burden of Tyre. Howl, ye ships of Tarshish; for it is laid waste, so that there is no house, no entering in: from the land of Chittim it is revealed to them.
verses.bsb: This is the burden against Tyre:  Wail, O ships of Tarshish, for Tyre is laid waste, without house or harbor. Word has reached them from the land of Cyprus.

verses.esv: Be still, O inhabitants of the coast; the merchants of Sidon, who cross the sea, have filled you.
verses.kjv: Be still, ye inhabitants of the isle; thou whom the merchants of Zidon, that pass over the sea, have replenished.
verses.bsb: Be silent, O dwellers of the coastland, you merchants of Sidon, whose traders have crossed the sea.

verses.esv: And on many waters your revenue was the grain of Shihor, the harvest of the Nile; you were the merchant of the nations.
verses.kjv: And by great waters the seed of Sihor, the harvest of the river, is her revenue; and she is a mart of nations.
verses.bsb: On the great waters came the grain of Shihor; the harvest of the Nile was the revenue of Tyre; she was the merchant of the nations.

verses.esv: Be ashamed, O Sidon, for the sea has spoken, the stronghold of the sea, saying: “I have neither labored nor given birth, I have neither reared young men nor brought up young women.”
verses.kjv: Be thou ashamed, O Zidon: for the sea hath spoken, even the strength of the sea, saying, I travail not, nor bring forth children, neither do I nourish up young men, nor bring up virgins.
verses.bsb: Be ashamed, O Sidon, the stronghold of the sea, for the sea has spoken: “I have not been in labor or given birth. I have not raised young men or brought up young women.”

verses.esv: When the report comes to Egypt, they will be in anguish over the report about Tyre.
verses.kjv: As at the report concerning Egypt, so shall they be sorely pained at the report of Tyre.
verses.bsb: When the report reaches Egypt, they will writhe in agony over the news of Tyre.

verses.esv: Cross over to Tarshish; wail, O inhabitants of the coast!
verses.kjv: Pass ye over to Tarshish; howl, ye inhabitants of the isle.
verses.bsb: Cross over to Tarshish; wail, O inhabitants of the coastland!

verses.esv: Is this your exultant city whose origin is from days of old, whose feet carried her to settle far away?
verses.kjv: Is this your joyous city, whose antiquity is of ancient days? her own feet shall carry her afar off to sojourn.
verses.bsb: Is this your jubilant city, whose origin is from antiquity, whose feet have taken her to settle far away?

verses.esv: Who has purposed this against Tyre, the bestower of crowns, whose merchants were princes, whose traders were the honored of the earth?
verses.kjv: Who hath taken this counsel against Tyre, the crowning city, whose merchants are princes, whose traffickers are the honourable of the earth?
verses.bsb: Who planned this against Tyre, the bestower of crowns, whose traders are princes, whose merchants are renowned on the earth?

verses.esv: The LORD of hosts has purposed it, to defile the pompous pride of all glory, to dishonor all the honored of the earth.
verses.kjv: The Lord of hosts hath purposed it, to stain the pride of all glory, and to bring into contempt all the honourable of the earth.
verses.bsb: The LORD of Hosts planned it, to defile all its glorious beauty, to disgrace all the renowned of the earth.

verses.esv: Cross over your land like the Nile, O daughter of Tarshish; there is no restraint anymore.
verses.kjv: Pass through thy land as a river, O daughter of Tarshish: there is no more strength.
verses.bsb: Cultivate your land like the Nile, O Daughter of Tarshish; there is no longer a harbor.

verses.esv: He has stretched out his hand over the sea; he has shaken the kingdoms; the LORD has given command concerning Canaan to destroy its strongholds.
verses.kjv: He stretched out his hand over the sea, he shook the kingdoms: the Lord hath given a commandment against the merchant city, to destroy the strong holds thereof.
verses.bsb: The LORD has stretched out His hand over the sea; He has made kingdoms tremble. He has given a command that the strongholds of Canaan be destroyed.

verses.esv: And he said: “You will no more exult, O oppressed virgin daughter of Sidon; arise, cross over to Cyprus, even there you will have no rest.”
verses.kjv: And he said, Thou shalt no more rejoice, O thou oppressed virgin, daughter of Zidon: arise, pass over to Chittim; there also shalt thou have no rest.
verses.bsb: He said, “You shall rejoice no more, O oppressed Virgin Daughter of Sidon. Get up and cross over to Cyprus— even there you will find no rest.”

verses.esv: Behold the land of the Chaldeans! This is the people that was not; Assyria destined it for wild beasts. They erected their siege towers, they stripped her palaces bare, they made her a ruin.
verses.kjv: Behold the land of the Chaldeans; this people was not, till the Assyrian founded it for them that dwell in the wilderness: they set up the towers thereof, they raised up the palaces thereof; and he brought it to ruin.
verses.bsb: Look at the land of the Chaldeans— a people now of no account. The Assyrians destined it for the desert creatures; they set up their siege towers and stripped its palaces. They brought it to ruin.

verses.esv: Wail, O ships of Tarshish, for your stronghold is laid waste.
verses.kjv: Howl, ye ships of Tarshish: for your strength is laid waste.
verses.bsb: Wail, O ships of Tarshish, for your harbor has been destroyed!

verses.esv: In that day Tyre will be forgotten for seventy years, like the days of one king. At the end of seventy years, it will happen to Tyre as in the song of the prostitute:
verses.kjv: And it shall come to pass in that day, that Tyre shall be forgotten seventy years, according to the days of one king: after the end of seventy years shall Tyre sing as an harlot.
verses.bsb: At that time Tyre will be forgotten for seventy years—the span of a king’s life. But at the end of seventy years, it will happen to Tyre as in the song of the harlot:

verses.esv: “Take a harp; go about the city, O forgotten prostitute! Make sweet melody; sing many songs, that you may be remembered.”
verses.kjv: Take an harp, go about the city, thou harlot that hast been forgotten; make sweet melody, sing many songs, that thou mayest be remembered.
verses.bsb: “Take up your harp, stroll through the city, O forgotten harlot. Make sweet melody, sing many a song, so you will be remembered.”

verses.esv: At the end of seventy years, the LORD will visit Tyre, and she will return to her wages and will prostitute herself with all the kingdoms of the world on the face of the earth.
verses.kjv: ¶ And it shall come to pass after the end of seventy years, that the Lord will visit Tyre, and she shall turn to her hire, and shall commit fornication with all the kingdoms of the world upon the face of the earth.
verses.bsb: And at the end of seventy years, the LORD will restore Tyre. Then she will return to hire as a prostitute and sell herself to all the kingdoms on the face of the earth.

verses.esv: Her merchandise and her wages will be holy to the LORD. It will not be stored or hoarded, but her merchandise will supply abundant food and fine clothing for those who dwell before the LORD.
verses.kjv: And her merchandise and her hire shall be holiness to the Lord: it shall not be treasured nor laid up; for her merchandise shall be for them that dwell before the Lord, to eat sufficiently, and for durable clothing.
verses.bsb: Yet her profits and wages will be set apart to the LORD; they will not be stored or saved, for her profit will go to those who live before the LORD, for abundant food and fine clothing.

verses.esv: Behold, the LORD will empty the earth and make it desolate, and he will twist its surface and scatter its inhabitants.
verses.kjv: BEHOLD, the Lord maketh the earth empty, and maketh it waste, and turneth it upside down, and scattereth abroad the inhabitants thereof.
verses.bsb: Behold, the LORD lays waste the earth and leaves it in ruins. He will twist its surface and scatter its inhabitants—

verses.esv: And it shall be, as with the people, so with the priest; as with the slave, so with his master; as with the maid, so with her mistress; as with the buyer, so with the seller; as with the lender, so with the borrower; as with the creditor, so with the debtor.
verses.kjv: And it shall be, as with the people, so with the priest; as with the servant, so with his master; as with the maid, so with her mistress; as with the buyer, so with the seller; as with the lender, so with the borrower; as with the taker of usury, so with the giver of usury to him.
verses.bsb: people and priest alike, servant and master, maid and mistress, buyer and seller, lender and borrower, creditor and debtor.

verses.esv: The earth shall be utterly empty and utterly plundered; for the LORD has spoken this word.
verses.kjv: The land shall be utterly emptied, and utterly spoiled: for the Lord hath spoken this word.
verses.bsb: The earth will be utterly laid waste and thoroughly plundered. For the LORD has spoken this word.

verses.esv: The earth mourns and withers; the world languishes and withers; the highest people of the earth languish.
verses.kjv: The earth mourneth and fadeth away, the world languisheth and fadeth away, the haughty people of the earth do languish.
verses.bsb: The earth mourns and withers; the world languishes and fades; the exalted of the earth waste away.

verses.esv: The earth lies defiled under its inhabitants; for they have transgressed the laws, violated the statutes, broken the everlasting covenant.
verses.kjv: The earth also is defiled under the inhabitants thereof; because they have transgressed the laws, changed the ordinance, broken the everlasting covenant.
verses.bsb: The earth is defiled by its people; they have transgressed the laws; they have overstepped the decrees and broken the everlasting covenant.

verses.esv: Therefore a curse devours the earth, and its inhabitants suffer for their guilt; therefore the inhabitants of the earth are scorched, and few men are left.
verses.kjv: Therefore hath the curse devoured the earth, and they that dwell therein are desolate: therefore the inhabitants of the earth are burned, and few men left.
verses.bsb: Therefore a curse has consumed the earth, and its inhabitants must bear the guilt; the earth’s dwellers have been burned, and only a few survive.

verses.esv: The wine mourns, the vine languishes, all the merry-hearted sigh.
verses.kjv: The new wine mourneth, the vine languisheth, all the merryhearted do sigh.
verses.bsb: The new wine dries up, the vine withers. All the merrymakers now groan.

verses.esv: The mirth of the tambourines is stilled, the noise of the jubilant has ceased, the mirth of the lyre is stilled.
verses.kjv: The mirth of tabrets ceaseth, the noise of them that rejoice endeth, the joy of the harp ceaseth.
verses.bsb: The joyful tambourines have ceased; the noise of revelers has stopped; the joyful harp is silent.

verses.esv: No more do they drink wine with singing; strong drink is bitter to those who drink it.
verses.kjv: They shall not drink wine with a song; strong drink shall be bitter to them that drink it.
verses.bsb: They no longer sing and drink wine; strong drink is bitter to those who consume it.

verses.esv: The wasted city is broken down; every house is shut up so that none can enter.
verses.kjv: The city of confusion is broken down: every house is shut up, that no man may come in.
verses.bsb: The city of chaos is shattered; every house is closed to entry.

verses.esv: There is an outcry in the streets for lack of wine; all joy has grown dark; the gladness of the earth is banished.
verses.kjv: There is a crying for wine in the streets; all joy is darkened, the mirth of the land is gone.
verses.bsb: In the streets they cry out for wine. All joy turns to gloom; rejoicing is exiled from the land.

verses.esv: Desolation is left in the city; the gates are battered into ruins.
verses.kjv: In the city is left desolation, and the gate is smitten with destruction.
verses.bsb: The city is left in ruins; its gate is reduced to rubble.

verses.esv: For thus it shall be in the midst of the earth among the nations, as when an olive tree is beaten, as at the gleaning when the grape harvest is done.
verses.kjv: ¶ When thus it shall be in the midst of the land among the people, there shall be as the shaking of an olive tree, and as the gleaning grapes when the vintage is done.
verses.bsb: So will it be on the earth and among the nations, like a harvested olive tree, like a gleaning after a grape harvest.

verses.esv: They lift up their voices, they sing for joy; over the majesty of the LORD they shout from the west.
verses.kjv: They shall lift up their voice, they shall sing for the majesty of the Lord, they shall cry aloud from the sea.
verses.bsb: They raise their voices, they shout for joy; from the west they proclaim the majesty of the LORD.

verses.esv: Therefore in the east give glory to the LORD; in the coastlands of the sea, give glory to the name of the LORD, the God of Israel.
verses.kjv: Wherefore glorify ye the Lord in the fires, even the name of the Lord God of Israel in the isles of the sea.
verses.bsb: Therefore glorify the LORD in the east. Extol the name of the LORD, the God of Israel in the islands of the sea.

verses.esv: From the ends of the earth we hear songs of praise, of glory to the Righteous One. But I say, “I waste away, I waste away. Woe is me! For the traitors have betrayed, with betrayal the traitors have betrayed.”
verses.kjv: ¶ From the uttermost part of the earth have we heard songs, even glory to the righteous. But I said, My leanness, my leanness, woe unto me! the treacherous dealers have dealt treacherously; yea, the treacherous dealers have dealt very treacherously.
verses.bsb: From the ends of the earth we hear singing: “Glory to the Righteous One.”   But I said, “I am wasting away! I am wasting away! Woe is me.” The treacherous betray; the treacherous deal in treachery.

verses.esv: Terror and the pit and the snare are upon you, O inhabitant of the earth!
verses.kjv: Fear, and the pit, and the snare, are upon thee, O inhabitant of the earth.
verses.bsb: Terror and pit and snare await you, O dweller of the earth.

verses.esv: He who flees at the sound of the terror shall fall into the pit, and he who climbs out of the pit shall be caught in the snare. For the windows of heaven are opened, and the foundations of the earth tremble.
verses.kjv: And it shall come to pass, that he who fleeth from the noise of the fear shall fall into the pit; and he that cometh up out of the midst of the pit shall be taken in the snare: for the windows from on high are open, and the foundations of the earth do shake.
verses.bsb: Whoever flees the sound of panic will fall into the pit, and whoever climbs from the pit will be caught in the snare.   For the windows of heaven are open, and the foundations of the earth are shaken.

verses.esv: The earth is utterly broken, the earth is split apart, the earth is violently shaken.
verses.kjv: The earth is utterly broken down, the earth is clean dissolved, the earth is moved exceedingly.
verses.bsb: The earth is utterly broken apart, the earth is split open, the earth is shaken violently.

verses.esv: The earth staggers like a drunken man; it sways like a hut; its transgression lies heavy upon it, and it falls, and will not rise again.
verses.kjv: The earth shall reel to and fro like a drunkard, and shall be removed like a cottage; and the transgression thereof shall be heavy upon it; and it shall fall, and not rise again.
verses.bsb: The earth staggers like a drunkard and sways like a shack. Earth’s rebellion weighs it down, and it falls, never to rise again.

verses.esv: On that day the LORD will punish the host of heaven, in heaven, and the kings of the earth, on the earth.
verses.kjv: And it shall come to pass in that day, that the Lord shall punish the host of the high ones that are on high, and the kings of the earth upon the earth.
verses.bsb: In that day the LORD will punish the host of heaven above and the kings of the earth below.

verses.esv: They will be gathered together as prisoners in a pit; they will be shut up in a prison, and after many days they will be punished.
verses.kjv: And they shall be gathered together, as prisoners are gathered in the pit, and shall be shut up in the prison, and after many days shall they be visited.
verses.bsb: They will be gathered together like prisoners in a pit. They will be confined to a dungeon and punished after many days.

verses.esv: Then the moon will be confounded and the sun ashamed, for the LORD of hosts reigns on Mount Zion and in Jerusalem, and his glory will be before his elders.
verses.kjv: Then the moon shall be confounded, and the sun ashamed, when the Lord of hosts shall reign in mount Zion, and in Jerusalem, and before his ancients gloriously.
verses.bsb: The moon will be confounded and the sun will be ashamed; for the LORD of Hosts will reign on Mount Zion and in Jerusalem, and before His elders with great glory.

verses.esv: O LORD, you are my God; I will exalt you; I will praise your name, for you have done wonderful things, plans formed of old, faithful and sure.
verses.kjv: O LORD, thou art my God; I will exalt thee, I will praise thy name; for thou hast done wonderful things; thy counsels of old are faithfulness and truth.
verses.bsb: O LORD, You are my God!   I will exalt You; I will praise Your name. For You have worked wonders— plans formed long ago— in perfect faithfulness.

verses.esv: For you have made the city a heap, the fortified city a ruin; the foreigners' palace is a city no more; it will never be rebuilt.
verses.kjv: For thou hast made of a city an heap; of a defenced city a ruin: a palace of strangers to be no city; it shall never be built.
verses.bsb: Indeed, You have made the city a heap of rubble, the fortified town a ruin. The fortress of strangers is a city no more; it will never be rebuilt.

verses.esv: Therefore strong peoples will glorify you; cities of ruthless nations will fear you.
verses.kjv: Therefore shall the strong people glorify thee, the city of the terrible nations shall fear thee.
verses.bsb: Therefore, a strong people will honor You. The cities of ruthless nations will revere You.

verses.esv: For you have been a stronghold to the poor, a stronghold to the needy in his distress, a shelter from the storm and a shade from the heat; for the breath of the ruthless is like a storm against a wall,
verses.kjv: For thou hast been a strength to the poor, a strength to the needy in his distress, a refuge from the storm, a shadow from the heat, when the blast of the terrible ones is as a storm against the wall.
verses.bsb: For You have been a refuge for the poor, a stronghold for the needy in distress, a refuge from the storm, a shade from the heat. For the breath of the ruthless is like rain against a wall,

verses.esv: like heat in a dry place. You subdue the noise of the foreigners; as heat by the shade of a cloud, so the song of the ruthless is put down.
verses.kjv: Thou shalt bring down the noise of strangers, as the heat in a dry place; even the heat with the shadow of a cloud: the branch of the terrible ones shall be brought low.
verses.bsb: like heat in a dry land. You subdue the uproar of foreigners. As the shade of a cloud cools the heat, so the song of the ruthless is silenced.

verses.esv: On this mountain the LORD of hosts will make for all peoples a feast of rich food, a feast of well-aged wine, of rich food full of marrow, of aged wine well refined.
verses.kjv: ¶ And in this mountain shall the Lord of hosts make unto all people a feast of fat things, a feast of wines on the lees, of fat things full of marrow, of wines on the lees well refined.
verses.bsb: On this mountain the LORD of Hosts will prepare a banquet for all the peoples, a feast of aged wine, of choice meat, of finely aged wine.

verses.esv: And he will swallow up on this mountain the covering that is cast over all peoples, the veil that is spread over all nations.
verses.kjv: And he will destroy in this mountain the face of the covering cast over all people, and the vail that is spread over all nations.
verses.bsb: On this mountain He will swallow up the shroud that enfolds all peoples, the sheet that covers all nations;

verses.esv: He will swallow up death forever; and the Lord GOD will wipe away tears from all faces, and the reproach of his people he will take away from all the earth, for the LORD has spoken.
verses.kjv: He will swallow up death in victory; and the Lord God will wipe away tears from off all faces; and the rebuke of his people shall he take away from off all the earth: for the Lord hath spoken it.
verses.bsb: He will swallow up death forever. The Lord GOD will wipe away the tears from every face and remove the disgrace of His people from the whole earth. For the LORD has spoken.

verses.esv: It will be said on that day, “Behold, this is our God; we have waited for him, that he might save us. This is the LORD; we have waited for him; let us be glad and rejoice in his salvation.”
verses.kjv: ¶ And it shall be said in that day, Lo, this is our God; we have waited for him, and he will save us: this is the Lord; we have waited for him, we will be glad and rejoice in his salvation.
verses.bsb: And in that day it will be said, “Surely this is our God; we have waited for Him, and He has saved us. This is the LORD for whom we have waited. Let us rejoice and be glad in His salvation.”

verses.esv: For the hand of the LORD will rest on this mountain, and Moab shall be trampled down in his place, as straw is trampled down in a dunghill.
verses.kjv: For in this mountain shall the hand of the Lord rest, and Moab shall be trodden down under him, even as straw is trodden down for the dunghill.
verses.bsb: For the hand of the LORD will rest on this mountain. But Moab will be trampled in his place as straw is trodden into the dung pile.

verses.esv: And he will spread out his hands in the midst of it as a swimmer spreads his hands out to swim, but the LORD will lay low his pompous pride together with the skill of his hands.
verses.kjv: And he shall spread forth his hands in the midst of them, as he that swimmeth spreadeth forth his hands to swim: and he shall bring down their pride together with the spoils of their hands.
verses.bsb: He will spread out his hands within it, as a swimmer spreads his arms to swim. His pride will be brought low, despite the skill of his hands.

verses.esv: And the high fortifications of his walls he will bring down, lay low, and cast to the ground, to the dust.
verses.kjv: And the fortress of the high fort of thy walls shall he bring down, lay low, and bring to the ground, even to the dust.
verses.bsb: The high-walled fortress will be brought down, cast to the ground, into the dust.

verses.esv: In that day this song will be sung in the land of Judah: “We have a strong city; he sets up salvation as walls and bulwarks.
verses.kjv: IN that day shall this song be sung in the land of Judah; We have a strong city; salvation will God appoint for walls and bulwarks.
verses.bsb: In that day this song will be sung in the land of Judah:  We have a strong city; salvation is established as its walls and ramparts.

verses.esv: Open the gates, that the righteous nation that keeps faith may enter in.
verses.kjv: Open ye the gates, that the righteous nation which keepeth the truth may enter in.
verses.bsb: Open the gates so a righteous nation may enter— one that remains faithful.

verses.esv: You keep him in perfect peace whose mind is stayed on you, because he trusts in you.
verses.kjv: Thou wilt keep him in perfect peace, whose mind is stayed on thee: because he trusteth in thee.
verses.bsb: You will keep in perfect peace the steadfast of mind, because he trusts in You.

verses.esv: Trust in the LORD forever, for the LORD GOD is an everlasting rock.
verses.kjv: Trust ye in the Lord for ever: for in the Lord JEHOVAH is everlasting strength:
verses.bsb: Trust in the LORD forever, because GOD the LORD is the Rock eternal.

verses.esv: For he has humbled the inhabitants of the height, the lofty city. He lays it low, lays it low to the ground, casts it to the dust.
verses.kjv: ¶ For he bringeth down them that dwell on high; the lofty city, he layeth it low; he layeth it low, even to the ground; he bringeth it even to the dust.
verses.bsb: For He has humbled those who dwell on high; He lays the lofty city low. He brings it down to the ground; He casts it into the dust.

verses.esv: The foot tramples it, the feet of the poor, the steps of the needy.”
verses.kjv: The foot shall tread it down, even the feet of the poor, and the steps of the needy.
verses.bsb: Feet trample it down— the feet of the oppressed, the steps of the poor.

verses.esv: The path of the righteous is level; you make level the way of the righteous.
verses.kjv: The way of the just is uprightness: thou, most upright, dost weigh the path of the just.
verses.bsb: The path of the righteous is level; You clear a straight path for the upright.

verses.esv: In the path of your judgments, O LORD, we wait for you; your name and remembrance are the desire of our soul.
verses.kjv: Yea, in the way of thy judgments, O Lord, have we waited for thee; the desire of our soul is to thy name, and to the remembrance of thee.
verses.bsb: Yes, we wait for You, O LORD; we walk in the path of Your judgments. Your name and renown are the desire of our souls.

verses.esv: My soul yearns for you in the night; my spirit within me earnestly seeks you. For when your judgments are in the earth, the inhabitants of the world learn righteousness.
verses.kjv: With my soul have I desired thee in the night; yea, with my spirit within me will I seek thee early: for when thy judgments are in the earth, the inhabitants of the world will learn righteousness.
verses.bsb: My soul longs for You in the night; indeed, my spirit seeks You at dawn. For when Your judgments come upon the earth, the people of the world learn righteousness.

verses.esv: If favor is shown to the wicked, he does not learn righteousness; in the land of uprightness he deals corruptly and does not see the majesty of the LORD.
verses.kjv: Let favour be shewed to the wicked, yet will he not learn righteousness: in the land of uprightness will he deal unjustly, and will not behold the majesty of the Lord.
verses.bsb: Though grace is shown to the wicked man, he does not learn righteousness. In the land of righteousness he acts unjustly and fails to see the majesty of the LORD.

verses.esv: O LORD, your hand is lifted up, but they do not see it. Let them see your zeal for your people, and be ashamed. Let the fire for your adversaries consume them.
verses.kjv: Lord, when thy hand is lifted up, they will not see: but they shall see, and be ashamed for their envy at the people; yea, the fire of thine enemies shall devour them.
verses.bsb: O LORD, Your hand is upraised, but they do not see it. They will see Your zeal for Your people and be put to shame. The fire set for Your enemies will consume them!

verses.esv: O LORD, you will ordain peace for us, for you have indeed done for us all our works.
verses.kjv: ¶ Lord, thou wilt ordain peace for us: for thou also hast wrought all our works in us.
verses.bsb: O LORD, You will establish peace for us. For all that we have accomplished, You have done for us.

verses.esv: O LORD our God, other lords besides you have ruled over us, but your name alone we bring to remembrance.
verses.kjv: O Lord our God, other lords beside thee have had dominion over us: but by thee only will we make mention of thy name.
verses.bsb: O LORD our God, other lords besides You have had dominion, but Your name alone do we confess.

verses.esv: They are dead, they will not live; they are shades, they will not arise; to that end you have visited them with destruction and wiped out all remembrance of them.
verses.kjv: They are dead, they shall not live; they are deceased, they shall not rise: therefore hast thou visited and destroyed them, and made all their memory to perish.
verses.bsb: The dead will not live; the departed spirits will not rise. Therefore You have punished and destroyed them; You have wiped out all memory of them.

verses.esv: But you have increased the nation, O LORD, you have increased the nation; you are glorified; you have enlarged all the borders of the land.
verses.kjv: Thou hast increased the nation, O Lord, thou hast increased the nation: thou art glorified: thou hadst removed it far unto all the ends of the earth.
verses.bsb: You have enlarged the nation, O LORD; You have enlarged the nation. You have gained glory for Yourself; You have extended all the borders of the land.

verses.esv: O LORD, in distress they sought you; they poured out a whispered prayer when your discipline was upon them.
verses.kjv: Lord, in trouble have they visited thee, they poured out a prayer when thy chastening was upon them.
verses.bsb: O LORD, they sought You in their distress; when You disciplined them, they poured out a quiet prayer.

verses.esv: Like a pregnant woman who writhes and cries out in her pangs when she is near to giving birth, so were we because of you, O LORD;
verses.kjv: Like as a woman with child, that draweth near the time of her delivery, is in pain, and crieth out in her pangs; so have we been in thy sight, O Lord.
verses.bsb: As a woman with child about to give birth writhes and cries out in pain, so were we in Your presence, O LORD.

verses.esv: we were pregnant, we writhed, but we have given birth to wind. We have accomplished no deliverance in the earth, and the inhabitants of the world have not fallen.
verses.kjv: We have been with child, we have been in pain, we have as it were brought forth wind; we have not wrought any deliverance in the earth; neither have the inhabitants of the world fallen.
verses.bsb: We were with child; we writhed in pain; but we gave birth to wind. We have given no salvation to the earth, nor brought any life into the world.

verses.esv: Your dead shall live; their bodies shall rise. You who dwell in the dust, awake and sing for joy! For your dew is a dew of light, and the earth will give birth to the dead.
verses.kjv: Thy dead men shall live, together with my dead body shall they arise. Awake and sing, ye that dwell in dust: for thy dew is as the dew of herbs, and the earth shall cast out the dead.
verses.bsb: Your dead will live; their bodies will rise. Awake and sing, you who dwell in the dust! For your dew is like the dew of the morning, and the earth will bring forth her dead.

verses.esv: Come, my people, enter your chambers, and shut your doors behind you; hide yourselves for a little while until the fury has passed by.
verses.kjv: ¶ Come, my people, enter thou into thy chambers, and shut thy doors about thee: hide thyself as it were for a little moment, until the indignation be overpast.
verses.bsb: Go, my people, enter your rooms and shut your doors behind you. Hide yourselves a little while until the wrath has passed.

verses.esv: For behold, the LORD is coming out from his place to punish the inhabitants of the earth for their iniquity, and the earth will disclose the blood shed on it, and will no more cover its slain.
verses.kjv: For, behold, the Lord cometh out of his place to punish the inhabitants of the earth for their iniquity: the earth also shall disclose her blood, and shall no more cover her slain.
verses.bsb: For behold, the LORD is coming out of His dwelling to punish the inhabitants of the earth for their iniquity. The earth will reveal her bloodshed and will no longer conceal her slain.

verses.esv: In that day the LORD with his hard and great and strong sword will punish Leviathan the fleeing serpent, Leviathan the twisting serpent, and he will slay the dragon that is in the sea.
verses.kjv: IN that day the Lord with his sore and great and strong sword shall punish leviathan the piercing serpent, even leviathan that crooked serpent; and he shall slay the dragon that is in the sea.
verses.bsb: In that day the LORD will take His sharp, great, and mighty sword, and bring judgment on Leviathan the fleeing serpent—Leviathan the coiling serpent—and He will slay the dragon of the sea.

verses.esv: In that day, “A pleasant vineyard, sing of it!
verses.kjv: In that day sing ye unto her, A vineyard of red wine.
verses.bsb: In that day:  “Sing about a fruitful vineyard.

verses.esv: I, the LORD, am its keeper; every moment I water it. Lest anyone punish it, I keep it night and day;
verses.kjv: I the Lord do keep it; I will water it every moment: lest any hurt it, I will keep it night and day.
verses.bsb: I, the LORD, am its keeper; I water it continually. I guard it night and day so no one can disturb it;

verses.esv: I have no wrath. Would that I had thorns and briers to battle! I would march against them, I would burn them up together.
verses.kjv: Fury is not in me: who would set the briers and thorns against me in battle? I would go through them, I would burn them together.
verses.bsb: I am not angry. If only thorns and briers confronted Me, I would march and trample them, I would burn them to the ground.

verses.esv: Or let them lay hold of my protection, let them make peace with me, let them make peace with me.”
verses.kjv: Or let him take hold of my strength, that he may make peace with me; and he shall make peace with me.
verses.bsb: Or let them lay claim to My protection; let them make peace with Me— yes, let them make peace with Me.”

verses.esv: In days to come Jacob shall take root, Israel shall blossom and put forth shoots and fill the whole world with fruit.
verses.kjv: He shall cause them that come of Jacob to take root: Israel shall blossom and bud, and fill the face of the world with fruit.
verses.bsb: In the days to come, Jacob will take root. Israel will bud and blossom and fill the whole world with fruit.

verses.esv: Has he struck them as he struck those who struck them? Or have they been slain as their slayers were slain?
verses.kjv: ¶ Hath he smitten him, as he smote those that smote him? or is he slain according to the slaughter of them that are slain by him?
verses.bsb: Has the LORD struck Israel as He struck her oppressors? Was she killed like those who slayed her?

verses.esv: Measure by measure, by exile you contended with them; he removed them with his fierce breath in the day of the east wind.
verses.kjv: In measure, when it shooteth forth, thou wilt debate with it: he stayeth his rough wind in the day of the east wind.
verses.bsb: By warfare and exile You contended with her and removed her with a fierce wind, as on the day the east wind blows.

verses.esv: Therefore by this the guilt of Jacob will be atoned for, and this will be the full fruit of the removal of his sin: when he makes all the stones of the altars like chalkstones crushed to pieces, no Asherim or incense altars will remain standing.
verses.kjv: By this therefore shall the iniquity of Jacob be purged; and this is all the fruit to take away his sin; when he maketh all the stones of the altar as chalkstones that are beaten in sunder, the groves and images shall not stand up.
verses.bsb: Therefore Jacob’s guilt will be atoned for, and the full fruit of the removal of his sin will be this: When he makes all the altar stones like crushed bits of chalk, no Asherah poles or incense altars will remain standing.

verses.esv: For the fortified city is solitary, a habitation deserted and forsaken, like the wilderness; there the calf grazes; there it lies down and strips its branches.
verses.kjv: Yet the defenced city shall be desolate, and the habitation forsaken, and left like a wilderness: there shall the calf feed, and there shall he lie down, and consume the branches thereof.
verses.bsb: For the fortified city lies deserted— a homestead abandoned, a wilderness forsaken. There the calves graze, and there they lie down; they strip its branches bare.

verses.esv: When its boughs are dry, they are broken; women come and make a fire of them. For this is a people without discernment; therefore he who made them will not have compassion on them; he who formed them will show them no favor.
verses.kjv: When the boughs thereof are withered, they shall be broken off: the women come, and set them on fire: for it is a people of no understanding: therefore he that made them will not have mercy on them, and he that formed them will shew them no favour.
verses.bsb: When its limbs are dry, they are broken off. Women come and use them for kindling; for this is a people without understanding. Therefore their Maker has no compassion on them, and their Creator shows them no favor.

verses.esv: In that day from the river Euphrates to the Brook of Egypt the LORD will thresh out the grain, and you will be gleaned one by one, O people of Israel.
verses.kjv: ¶ And it shall come to pass in that day, that the Lord shall beat off from the channel of the river unto the stream of Egypt, and ye shall be gathered one by one, O ye children of Israel.
verses.bsb: In that day the LORD will thresh from the flowing Euphrates to the Wadi of Egypt, and you, O Israelites, will be gathered one by one.

verses.esv: And in that day a great trumpet will be blown, and those who were lost in the land of Assyria and those who were driven out to the land of Egypt will come and worship the LORD on the holy mountain at Jerusalem.
verses.kjv: And it shall come to pass in that day, that the great trumpet shall be blown, and they shall come which were ready to perish in the land of Assyria, and the outcasts in the land of Egypt, and shall worship the Lord in the holy mount at Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: And in that day a great ram’s horn will sound, and those who were perishing in Assyria will come forth with those who were exiles in Egypt. And they will worship the LORD on the holy mountain in Jerusalem.

verses.esv: Ah, the proud crown of the drunkards of Ephraim, and the fading flower of its glorious beauty, which is on the head of the rich valley of those overcome with wine!
verses.kjv: WOE to the crown of pride, to the drunkards of Ephraim, whose glorious beauty is a fading flower, which are on the head of the fat valleys of them that are overcome with wine!
verses.bsb: Woe to the majestic crown of Ephraim’s drunkards, to the fading flower of his glorious splendor, set on the summit above the fertile valley, the pride of those overcome by wine.

verses.esv: Behold, the Lord has one who is mighty and strong; like a storm of hail, a destroying tempest, like a storm of mighty, overflowing waters, he casts down to the earth with his hand.
verses.kjv: Behold, the Lord hath a mighty and strong one, which as a tempest of hail and a destroying storm, as a flood of mighty waters overflowing, shall cast down to the earth with the hand.
verses.bsb: Behold, the Lord has one who is strong and mighty. Like a hailstorm or destructive tempest, like a driving rain or flooding downpour, he will smash that crown to the ground.

verses.esv: The proud crown of the drunkards of Ephraim will be trodden underfoot;
verses.kjv: The crown of pride, the drunkards of Ephraim, shall be trodden under feet:
verses.bsb: The majestic crown of Ephraim’s drunkards will be trampled underfoot.

verses.esv: and the fading flower of its glorious beauty, which is on the head of the rich valley, will be like a first-ripe fig before the summer: when someone sees it, he swallows it as soon as it is in his hand.
verses.kjv: And the glorious beauty, which is on the head of the fat valley, shall be a fading flower, and as the hasty fruit before the summer; which when he that looketh upon it seeth, while it is yet in his hand he eateth it up.
verses.bsb: The fading flower of his beautiful splendor, set on the summit above the fertile valley, will be like a ripe fig before the summer harvest: Whoever sees it will take it in his hand and swallow it.

verses.esv: In that day the LORD of hosts will be a crown of glory, and a diadem of beauty, to the remnant of his people,
verses.kjv: ¶ In that day shall the Lord of hosts be for a crown of glory, and for a diadem of beauty, unto the residue of his people,
verses.bsb: On that day the LORD of Hosts will be a crown of glory, a diadem of splendor to the remnant of His people,

verses.esv: and a spirit of justice to him who sits in judgment, and strength to those who turn back the battle at the gate.
verses.kjv: And for a spirit of judgment to him that sitteth in judgment, and for strength to them that turn the battle to the gate.
verses.bsb: a spirit of justice to him who sits in judgment, and a strength to those who repel the onslaught at the gate.

verses.esv: These also reel with wine and stagger with strong drink; the priest and the prophet reel with strong drink, they are swallowed by wine, they stagger with strong drink, they reel in vision, they stumble in giving judgment.
verses.kjv: ¶ But they also have erred through wine, and through strong drink are out of the way; the priest and the prophet have erred through strong drink, they are swallowed up of wine, they are out of the way through strong drink; they err in vision, they stumble in judgment.
verses.bsb: These also stagger from wine and stumble from strong drink: Priests and prophets reel from strong drink and are befuddled by wine. They stumble because of strong drink, muddled in their visions and stumbling in their judgments.

verses.esv: For all tables are full of filthy vomit, with no space left.
verses.kjv: For all tables are full of vomit and filthiness, so that there is no place clean.
verses.bsb: For all their tables are covered with vomit; there is not a place without filth.

verses.esv: “To whom will he teach knowledge, and to whom will he explain the message? Those who are weaned from the milk, those taken from the breast?
verses.kjv: ¶ Whom shall he teach knowledge? and whom shall he make to understand doctrine? them that are weaned from the milk, and drawn from the breasts.
verses.bsb: Whom is He trying to teach? To whom is He explaining His message? To infants just weaned from milk? To babies removed from the breast?

verses.esv: For it is precept upon precept, precept upon precept, line upon line, line upon line, here a little, there a little.”
verses.kjv: For precept must be upon precept, precept upon precept; line upon line, line upon line; here a little, and there a little:
verses.bsb: For they hear: “Order on order, order on order, line on line, line on line; a little here, a little there.”

verses.esv: For by people of strange lips and with a foreign tongue the LORD will speak to this people,
verses.kjv: For with stammering lips and another tongue will he speak to this people.
verses.bsb: Indeed, with mocking lips and foreign tongues, He will speak to this people

verses.esv: to whom he has said, “This is rest; give rest to the weary; and this is repose”; yet they would not hear.
verses.kjv: To whom he said, This is the rest wherewith ye may cause the weary to rest; and this is the refreshing: yet they would not hear.
verses.bsb: to whom He has said: “This is the place of rest, let the weary rest; this is the place of repose.”   But they would not listen.

verses.esv: And the word of the LORD will be to them precept upon precept, precept upon precept, line upon line, line upon line, here a little, there a little, that they may go, and fall backward, and be broken, and snared, and taken.
verses.kjv: But the word of the Lord was unto them precept upon precept, precept upon precept; line upon line, line upon line; here a little, and there a little; that they might go, and fall backward, and be broken, and snared, and taken.
verses.bsb: Then the word of the LORD to them will become: “Order on order, order on order, line on line, line on line; a little here, a little there,” so that they will go stumbling backward and will be injured, ensnared, and captured.

verses.esv: Therefore hear the word of the LORD, you scoffers, who rule this people in Jerusalem!
verses.kjv: ¶ Wherefore hear the word of the Lord, ye scornful men, that rule this people which is in Jerusalem.
verses.bsb: Therefore hear the word of the LORD, O scoffers who rule this people in Jerusalem.

verses.esv: Because you have said, “We have made a covenant with death, and with Sheol we have an agreement, when the overwhelming whip passes through it will not come to us, for we have made lies our refuge, and in falsehood we have taken shelter”;
verses.kjv: Because ye have said, We have made a covenant with death, and with hell are we at agreement; when the overflowing scourge shall pass through, it shall not come unto us: for we have made lies our refuge, and under falsehood have we hid ourselves:
verses.bsb: For you said, “We have made a covenant with death; we have fashioned an agreement with Sheol. When the overwhelming scourge passes through it will not touch us, because we have made lies our refuge and falsehood our hiding place.”

verses.esv: therefore thus says the Lord GOD, “Behold, I am the one who has laid as a foundation in Zion, a stone, a tested stone, a precious cornerstone, of a sure foundation: ‘Whoever believes will not be in haste.’
verses.kjv: ¶ Therefore thus saith the Lord God, Behold, I lay in Zion for a foundation a stone, a tried stone, a precious corner stone, a sure foundation: he that believeth shall not make haste.
verses.bsb: So this is what the Lord GOD says:  “See, I lay a stone in Zion, a tested stone, a precious cornerstone, a sure foundation; the one who believes will never be shaken.

verses.esv: And I will make justice the line, and righteousness the plumb line; and hail will sweep away the refuge of lies, and waters will overwhelm the shelter.”
verses.kjv: Judgment also will I lay to the line, and righteousness to the plummet: and the hail shall sweep away the refuge of lies, and the waters shall overflow the hiding place.
verses.bsb: I will make justice the measuring line and righteousness the level. Hail will sweep away your refuge of lies, and water will flood your hiding place.

verses.esv: Then your covenant with death will be annulled, and your agreement with Sheol will not stand; when the overwhelming scourge passes through, you will be beaten down by it.
verses.kjv: ¶ And your covenant with death shall be disannulled, and your agreement with hell shall not stand; when the overflowing scourge shall pass through, then ye shall be trodden down by it.
verses.bsb: Your covenant with death will be dissolved, and your agreement with Sheol will not stand. When the overwhelming scourge passes through, you will be trampled by it.

verses.esv: As often as it passes through it will take you; for morning by morning it will pass through, by day and by night; and it will be sheer terror to understand the message.
verses.kjv: From the time that it goeth forth it shall take you: for morning by morning shall it pass over, by day and by night: and it shall be a vexation only to understand the report.
verses.bsb: As often as it passes through, it will carry you away; it will sweep through morning after morning, by day and by night.”   The understanding of this message will bring sheer terror.

verses.esv: For the bed is too short to stretch oneself on, and the covering too narrow to wrap oneself in.
verses.kjv: For the bed is shorter than that a man can stretch himself on it: and the covering narrower than that he can wrap himself in it.
verses.bsb: Indeed, the bed is too short to stretch out on, and the blanket too small to wrap around you.

verses.esv: For the LORD will rise up as on Mount Perazim; as in the Valley of Gibeon he will be roused; to do his deed—strange is his deed! and to work his work—alien is his work!
verses.kjv: For the Lord shall rise up as in mount Perazim, he shall be wroth as in the valley of Gibeon, that he may do his work, his strange work; and bring to pass his act, his strange act.
verses.bsb: For the LORD will rise up as at Mount Perazim. He will rouse Himself as in the Valley of Gibeon, to do His work, His strange work, and to perform His task, His disturbing task.

verses.esv: Now therefore do not scoff, lest your bonds be made strong; for I have heard a decree of destruction from the Lord GOD of hosts against the whole land.
verses.kjv: Now therefore be ye not mockers, lest your bands be made strong: for I have heard from the Lord God of hosts a consumption, even determined upon the whole earth.
verses.bsb: So now, do not mock, or your shackles will become heavier. Indeed, I have heard from the Lord GOD of Hosts a decree of destruction against the whole land.

verses.esv: Give ear, and hear my voice; give attention, and hear my speech.
verses.kjv: ¶ Give ye ear, and hear my voice; hearken, and hear my speech.
verses.bsb: Listen and hear my voice. Pay attention and hear what I say.

verses.esv: Does he who plows for sowing plow continually? Does he continually open and harrow his ground?
verses.kjv: Doth the plowman plow all day to sow? doth he open and break the clods of his ground?
verses.bsb: Does the plowman plow for planting every day? Does he continuously loosen and harrow the soil?

verses.esv: When he has leveled its surface, does he not scatter dill, sow cumin, and put in wheat in rows and barley in its proper place, and emmer as the border?
verses.kjv: When he hath made plain the face thereof, doth he not cast abroad the fitches, and scatter the cummin, and cast in the principal wheat and the appointed barley and the rie in their place?
verses.bsb: When he has leveled its surface, does he not sow caraway and scatter cumin? He plants wheat in rows and barley in plots, and rye within its border.

verses.esv: For he is rightly instructed; his God teaches him.
verses.kjv: For his God doth instruct him to discretion, and doth teach him.
verses.bsb: For his God instructs and teaches him properly.

verses.esv: Dill is not threshed with a threshing sledge, nor is a cart wheel rolled over cumin, but dill is beaten out with a stick, and cumin with a rod.
verses.kjv: For the fitches are not threshed with a threshing instrument, neither is a cart wheel turned about upon the cummin; but the fitches are beaten out with a staff, and the cummin with a rod.
verses.bsb: Surely caraway is not threshed with a sledge, and the wheel of a cart is not rolled over the cumin. But caraway is beaten out with a stick, and cumin with a rod.

verses.esv: Does one crush grain for bread? No, he does not thresh it forever; when he drives his cart wheel over it with his horses, he does not crush it.
verses.kjv: Bread corn is bruised; because he will not ever be threshing it, nor break it with the wheel of his cart, nor bruise it with his horsemen.
verses.bsb: Grain for bread must be ground, but it is not endlessly threshed. Though the wheels of the cart roll over it, the horses do not crush it.

verses.esv: This also comes from the LORD of hosts; he is wonderful in counsel and excellent in wisdom.
verses.kjv: This also cometh forth from the Lord of hosts, which is wonderful in counsel, and excellent in working.
verses.bsb: This also comes from the LORD of Hosts, who is wonderful in counsel and excellent in wisdom.

verses.esv: Ah, Ariel, Ariel, the city where David encamped! Add year to year; let the feasts run their round.
verses.kjv: WOE to Ariel, to Ariel, the city where David dwelt! add ye year to year; let them kill sacrifices.
verses.bsb: Woe to you, O Ariel, the city of Ariel where David camped! Year upon year let your festivals recur.

verses.esv: Yet I will distress Ariel, and there shall be moaning and lamentation, and she shall be to me like an Ariel.
verses.kjv: Yet I will distress Ariel, and there shall be heaviness and sorrow: and it shall be unto me as Ariel.
verses.bsb: And I will constrain Ariel, and there will be mourning and lamentation; she will be like an altar hearth before Me.

verses.esv: And I will encamp against you all around, and will besiege you with towers and I will raise siegeworks against you.
verses.kjv: And I will camp against thee round about, and will lay siege against thee with a mount, and I will raise forts against thee.
verses.bsb: I will camp in a circle around you; I will besiege you with towers and set up siege works against you.
