verses.bsb: Surely you have heard what the kings of Assyria have done to all the other countries, devoting them to destruction. Will you then be spared?

verses.esv: Have the gods of the nations delivered them, the nations that my fathers destroyed, Gozan, Haran, Rezeph, and the people of Eden who were in Telassar?
verses.kjv: Have the gods of the nations delivered them which my fathers have destroyed, as Gozan, and Haran, and Rezeph, and the children of Eden which were in Telassar?
verses.bsb: Did the gods of the nations destroyed by my fathers rescue those nations—the gods of Gozan, Haran, and Rezeph, and of the people of Eden in Telassar?

verses.esv: Where is the king of Hamath, the king of Arpad, the king of the city of Sepharvaim, the king of Hena, or the king of Ivvah?’”
verses.kjv: Where is the king of Hamath, and the king of Arphad, and the king of the city of Sepharvaim, Hena, and Ivah?
verses.bsb: Where are the kings of Hamath, Arpad, Sepharvaim, Hena, and Ivvah?’”

verses.esv: Hezekiah received the letter from the hand of the messengers, and read it; and Hezekiah went up to the house of the LORD, and spread it before the LORD.
verses.kjv: ¶ And Hezekiah received the letter from the hand of the messengers, and read it: and Hezekiah went up unto the house of the Lord, and spread it before the Lord.
verses.bsb: So Hezekiah received the letter from the messengers, read it, and went up to the house of the LORD and spread it out before the LORD.

verses.esv: And Hezekiah prayed to the LORD:
verses.kjv: And Hezekiah prayed unto the Lord, saying,
verses.bsb: And Hezekiah prayed to the LORD:

verses.esv: “O LORD of hosts, God of Israel, enthroned above the cherubim, you are the God, you alone, of all the kingdoms of the earth; you have made heaven and earth.
verses.kjv: O Lord of hosts, God of Israel, that dwellest between the cherubims, thou art the God, even thou alone, of all the kingdoms of the earth: thou hast made heaven and earth.
verses.bsb: “O LORD of Hosts, God of Israel, enthroned above the cherubim, You alone are God over all the kingdoms of the earth. You made the heavens and the earth.

verses.esv: Incline your ear, O LORD, and hear; open your eyes, O LORD, and see; and hear all the words of Sennacherib, which he has sent to mock the living God.
verses.kjv: Incline thine ear, O Lord, and hear; open thine eyes, O Lord, and see: and hear all the words of Sennacherib, which hath sent to reproach the living God.
verses.bsb: Incline Your ear, O LORD, and hear; open Your eyes, O LORD, and see. Listen to all the words that Sennacherib has sent to defy the living God.

verses.esv: Truly, O LORD, the kings of Assyria have laid waste all the nations and their lands,
verses.kjv: Of a truth, Lord, the kings of Assyria have laid waste all the nations, and their countries,
verses.bsb: Truly, O LORD, the kings of Assyria have laid waste all these countries and their lands.

verses.esv: and have cast their gods into the fire. For they were no gods, but the work of men's hands, wood and stone. Therefore they were destroyed.
verses.kjv: And have cast their gods into the fire: for they were no gods, but the work of men’s hands, wood and stone: therefore they have destroyed them.
verses.bsb: They have cast their gods into the fire and destroyed them, for they were not gods, but only wood and stone—the work of human hands.

verses.esv: So now, O LORD our God, save us from his hand, that all the kingdoms of the earth may know that you alone are the LORD.”
verses.kjv: Now therefore, O Lord our God, save us from his hand, that all the kingdoms of the earth may know that thou art the Lord, even thou only.
verses.bsb: And now, O LORD our God, save us from his hand, so that all the kingdoms of the earth may know that You alone, O LORD, are God.”

verses.esv: Then Isaiah the son of Amoz sent to Hezekiah, saying, “Thus says the LORD, the God of Israel: Because you have prayed to me concerning Sennacherib king of Assyria,
verses.kjv: ¶ Then Isaiah the son of Amoz sent unto Hezekiah, saying, Thus saith the Lord God of Israel, Whereas thou hast prayed to me against Sennacherib king of Assyria:
verses.bsb: Then Isaiah son of Amoz sent a message to Hezekiah: “This is what the LORD, the God of Israel, says: Because you have prayed to Me concerning Sennacherib king of Assyria,

verses.esv: this is the word that the LORD has spoken concerning him: “‘She despises you, she scorns you— the virgin daughter of Zion; she wags her head behind you— the daughter of Jerusalem.
verses.kjv: This is the word which the Lord hath spoken concerning him; The virgin, the daughter of Zion, hath despised thee, and laughed thee to scorn; the daughter of Jerusalem hath shaken her head at thee.
verses.bsb: this is the word that the LORD has spoken against him:  ‘The Virgin Daughter of Zion despises you and mocks you; the Daughter of Jerusalem shakes her head behind you.

verses.esv: “‘Whom have you mocked and reviled? Against whom have you raised your voice and lifted your eyes to the heights? Against the Holy One of Israel!
verses.kjv: Whom hast thou reproached and blasphemed? and against whom hast thou exalted thy voice, and lifted up thine eyes on high? even against the Holy One of Israel.
verses.bsb: Whom have you taunted and blasphemed? Against whom have you raised your voice and lifted your eyes in pride? Against the Holy One of Israel!

verses.esv: By your servants you have mocked the Lord, and you have said, With my many chariots I have gone up the heights of the mountains, to the far recesses of Lebanon, to cut down its tallest cedars, its choicest cypresses, to come to its remotest height, its most fruitful forest.
verses.kjv: By thy servants hast thou reproached the Lord, and hast said, By the multitude of my chariots am I come up to the height of the mountains, to the sides of Lebanon; and I will cut down the tall cedars thereof, and the choice fir trees thereof: and I will enter into the height of his border, and the forest of his Carmel.
verses.bsb: Through your servants you have taunted the Lord, and you have said: “With my many chariots I have ascended to the heights of the mountains, to the remote peaks of Lebanon. I have cut down its tallest cedars, the finest of its cypresses. I have reached its farthest heights, the densest of its forests.

verses.esv: I dug wells and drank waters, to dry up with the sole of my foot all the streams of Egypt.
verses.kjv: I have digged, and drunk water; and with the sole of my feet have I dried up all the rivers of the besieged places.
verses.bsb: I have dug wells and drunk foreign waters. With the soles of my feet I have dried up all the streams of Egypt.”

verses.esv: “‘Have you not heard that I determined it long ago? I planned from days of old what now I bring to pass, that you should make fortified cities crash into heaps of ruins,
verses.kjv: Hast thou not heard long ago, how I have done it; and of ancient times, that I have formed it? now have I brought it to pass, that thou shouldest be to lay waste defenced cities into ruinous heaps.
verses.bsb: Have you not heard? Long ago I ordained it; in days of old I planned it. Now I have brought it to pass, that you should crush fortified cities into piles of rubble.

verses.esv: while their inhabitants, shorn of strength, are dismayed and confounded, and have become like plants of the field and like tender grass, like grass on the housetops, blighted before it is grown.
verses.kjv: Therefore their inhabitants were of small power, they were dismayed and confounded: they were as the grass of the field, and as the green herb, as the grass on the housetops, and as corn blasted before it be grown up.
verses.bsb: Therefore their inhabitants, devoid of power, are dismayed and ashamed. They are like plants in the field, tender green shoots, grass on the rooftops, scorched before it is grown.

verses.esv: “‘I know your sitting down and your going out and coming in, and your raging against me.
verses.kjv: But I know thy abode, and thy going out, and thy coming in, and thy rage against me.
verses.bsb: But I know your sitting down, your going out and coming in, and your raging against Me.

verses.esv: Because you have raged against me and your complacency has come to my ears, I will put my hook in your nose and my bit in your mouth, and I will turn you back on the way by which you came.’
verses.kjv: Because thy rage against me, and thy tumult, is come up into mine ears, therefore will I put my hook in thy nose, and my bridle in thy lips, and I will turn thee back by the way by which thou camest.
verses.bsb: Because your rage and arrogance against Me have reached My ears, I will put My hook in your nose and My bit in your mouth; I will send you back the way you came.’

verses.esv: “And this shall be the sign for you: this year you shall eat what grows of itself, and in the second year what springs from that. Then in the third year sow and reap, and plant vineyards, and eat their fruit.
verses.kjv: And this shall be a sign unto thee, Ye shall eat this year such as groweth of itself; and the second year that which springeth of the same: and in the third year sow ye, and reap, and plant vineyards, and eat the fruit thereof.
verses.bsb: And this will be a sign to you, O Hezekiah:  This year you will eat what grows on its own, and in the second year what springs from the same. But in the third year you will sow and reap; you will plant vineyards and eat their fruit.

verses.esv: And the surviving remnant of the house of Judah shall again take root downward and bear fruit upward.
verses.kjv: And the remnant that is escaped of the house of Judah shall again take root downward, and bear fruit upward:
verses.bsb: And the surviving remnant of the house of Judah will again take root below and bear fruit above.

verses.esv: For out of Jerusalem shall go a remnant, and out of Mount Zion a band of survivors. The zeal of the LORD of hosts will do this.
verses.kjv: For out of Jerusalem shall go forth a remnant, and they that escape out of mount Zion: the zeal of the Lord of hosts shall do this.
verses.bsb: For a remnant will go forth from Jerusalem, and survivors from Mount Zion. The zeal of the LORD of Hosts will accomplish this.

verses.esv: “Therefore thus says the LORD concerning the king of Assyria: He shall not come into this city or shoot an arrow there or come before it with a shield or cast up a siege mound against it.
verses.kjv: Therefore thus saith the Lord concerning the king of Assyria, He shall not come into this city, nor shoot an arrow there, nor come before it with shields, nor cast a bank against it.
verses.bsb: So this is what the LORD says about the king of Assyria:  ‘He will not enter this city or shoot an arrow into it. He will not come before it with a shield or build up a siege ramp against it.

verses.esv: By the way that he came, by the same he shall return, and he shall not come into this city, declares the LORD.
verses.kjv: By the way that he came, by the same shall he return, and shall not come into this city, saith the Lord.
verses.bsb: He will go back the way he came, and he will not enter this city,’ declares the LORD.

verses.esv: For I will defend this city to save it, for my own sake and for the sake of my servant David.”
verses.kjv: For I will defend this city to save it for mine own sake, and for my servant David’s sake.
verses.bsb: ‘I will defend this city and save it for My own sake and for the sake of My servant David.’”

verses.esv: And the angel of the LORD went out and struck down 185,000 in the camp of the Assyrians. And when people arose early in the morning, behold, these were all dead bodies.
verses.kjv: Then the angel of the Lord went forth, and smote in the camp of the Assyrians a hundred and fourscore and five thousand: and when they arose early in the morning, behold, they were all dead corpses.
verses.bsb: Then the angel of the LORD went out and struck down 185,000 men in the camp of the Assyrians. When the people got up the next morning, there were all the dead bodies!

verses.esv: Then Sennacherib king of Assyria departed and returned home and lived at Nineveh.
verses.kjv: ¶ So Sennacherib king of Assyria departed, and went and returned, and dwelt at Nineveh.
verses.bsb: So Sennacherib king of Assyria broke camp and withdrew. He returned to Nineveh and stayed there.

verses.esv: And as he was worshiping in the house of Nisroch his god, Adrammelech and Sharezer, his sons, struck him down with the sword. And after they escaped into the land of Ararat, Esarhaddon his son reigned in his place.
verses.kjv: And it came to pass, as he was worshipping in the house of Nisroch his god, that Adrammelech and Sharezer his sons smote him with the sword; and they escaped into the land of Armenia: and Esar-haddon his son reigned in his stead.
verses.bsb: One day, while he was worshiping in the temple of his god Nisroch, his sons Adrammelech and Sharezer put him to the sword and escaped to the land of Ararat. And his son Esar-haddon reigned in his place.

verses.esv: In those days Hezekiah became sick and was at the point of death. And Isaiah the prophet the son of Amoz came to him, and said to him, “Thus says the LORD: Set your house in order, for you shall die, you shall not recover.”
verses.kjv: IN those days was Hezekiah sick unto death. And Isaiah the prophet the son of Amoz came unto him, and said unto him, Thus saith the Lord, Set thine house in order: for thou shalt die, and not live.
verses.bsb: In those days Hezekiah became mortally ill. The prophet Isaiah son of Amoz came to him and said, “This is what the LORD says: ‘Put your house in order, for you are about to die; you will not recover.’”

verses.esv: Then Hezekiah turned his face to the wall and prayed to the LORD,
verses.kjv: Then Hezekiah turned his face toward the wall, and prayed unto the Lord,
verses.bsb: Then Hezekiah turned his face to the wall and prayed to the LORD,

verses.esv: and said, “Please, O LORD, remember how I have walked before you in faithfulness and with a whole heart, and have done what is good in your sight.” And Hezekiah wept bitterly.
verses.kjv: And said, Remember now, O Lord, I beseech thee, how I have walked before thee in truth and with a perfect heart, and have done that which is good in thy sight. And Hezekiah wept sore.
verses.bsb: saying, “Please, O LORD, remember how I have walked before You faithfully and with wholehearted devotion; I have done what was good in Your sight.” And Hezekiah wept bitterly.

verses.esv: Then the word of the LORD came to Isaiah:
verses.kjv: ¶ Then came the word of the Lord to Isaiah, saying,
verses.bsb: And the word of the LORD came to Isaiah, saying,

verses.esv: “Go and say to Hezekiah, Thus says the LORD, the God of David your father: I have heard your prayer; I have seen your tears. Behold, I will add fifteen years to your life.
verses.kjv: Go, and say to Hezekiah, Thus saith the Lord, the God of David thy father, I have heard thy prayer, I have seen thy tears: behold, I will add unto thy days fifteen years.
verses.bsb: “Go and tell Hezekiah that this is what the LORD, the God of your father David, says: ‘I have heard your prayer; I have seen your tears. Behold, I will add fifteen years to your life.

verses.esv: I will deliver you and this city out of the hand of the king of Assyria, and will defend this city.
verses.kjv: And I will deliver thee and this city out of the hand of the king of Assyria: and I will defend this city.
verses.bsb: And I will deliver you and this city from the hand of the king of Assyria. I will defend this city.

verses.esv: “This shall be the sign to you from the LORD, that the LORD will do this thing that he has promised:
verses.kjv: And this shall be a sign unto thee from the Lord, that the Lord will do this thing that he hath spoken;
verses.bsb: This will be a sign to you from the LORD that He will do what He has promised:

verses.esv: Behold, I will make the shadow cast by the declining sun on the dial of Ahaz turn back ten steps.” So the sun turned back on the dial the ten steps by which it had declined.
verses.kjv: Behold, I will bring again the shadow of the degrees, which is gone down in the sun dial of Ahaz, ten degrees backward. So the sun returned ten degrees, by which degrees it was gone down.
verses.bsb: I will make the sun’s shadow that falls on the stairway of Ahaz go back ten steps.’”  So the sunlight went back the ten steps it had descended.

verses.esv: A writing of Hezekiah king of Judah, after he had been sick and had recovered from his sickness:
verses.kjv: ¶ The writing of Hezekiah king of Judah, when he had been sick, and was recovered of his sickness:
verses.bsb: This is a writing by Hezekiah king of Judah after his illness and recovery:

verses.esv: I said, In the middle of my days I must depart; I am consigned to the gates of Sheol for the rest of my years.
verses.kjv: I said in the cutting off of my days, I shall go to the gates of the grave: I am deprived of the residue of my years.
verses.bsb: I said, “In the prime of my life I must go through the gates of Sheol and be deprived of the remainder of my years.”

verses.esv: I said, I shall not see the LORD, the LORD in the land of the living; I shall look on man no more among the inhabitants of the world.
verses.kjv: I said, I shall not see the Lord, even the Lord, in the land of the living: I shall behold man no more with the inhabitants of the world.
verses.bsb: I said, “I will never again see the LORD, even the LORD, in the land of the living; I will no longer look on mankind with those who dwell in this world.

verses.esv: My dwelling is plucked up and removed from me like a shepherd's tent; like a weaver I have rolled up my life; he cuts me off from the loom; from day to night you bring me to an end;
verses.kjv: Mine age is departed, and is removed from me as a shepherd’s tent: I have cut off like a weaver my life: he will cut me off with pining sickness: from day even to night wilt thou make an end of me.
verses.bsb: My dwelling has been picked up and removed from me like a shepherd’s tent. I have rolled up my life like a weaver; He cuts me off from the loom; from day until night You make an end of me.

verses.esv: I calmed myself until morning; like a lion he breaks all my bones; from day to night you bring me to an end.
verses.kjv: I reckoned till morning, that, as a lion, so will he break all my bones: from day even to night wilt thou make an end of me.
verses.bsb: I composed myself until the morning. Like a lion He breaks all my bones; from day until night You make an end of me.

verses.esv: Like a swallow or a crane I chirp; I moan like a dove. My eyes are weary with looking upward. O Lord, I am oppressed; be my pledge of safety!
verses.kjv: Like a crane or a swallow, so did I chatter: I did mourn as a dove: mine eyes fail with looking upward: O Lord, I am oppressed; undertake for me.
verses.bsb: I chirp like a swallow or crane; I moan like a dove. My eyes grow weak as I look upward. O Lord, I am oppressed; be my security.”

verses.esv: What shall I say? For he has spoken to me, and he himself has done it. I walk slowly all my years because of the bitterness of my soul.
verses.kjv: What shall I say? he hath both spoken unto me, and himself hath done it: I shall go softly all my years in the bitterness of my soul.
verses.bsb: What can I say? He has spoken to me, and He Himself has done this. I will walk slowly all my years because of the anguish of my soul.

verses.esv: O Lord, by these things men live, and in all these is the life of my spirit. Oh restore me to health and make me live!
verses.kjv: O Lord, by these things men live, and in all these things is the life of my spirit: so wilt thou recover me, and make me to live.
verses.bsb: O Lord, by such things men live, and in all of them my spirit finds life. You have restored me to health and have let me live.

verses.esv: Behold, it was for my welfare that I had great bitterness; but in love you have delivered my life from the pit of destruction, for you have cast all my sins behind your back.
verses.kjv: Behold, for peace I had great bitterness: but thou hast in love to my soul delivered it from the pit of corruption: for thou hast cast all my sins behind thy back.
verses.bsb: Surely for my own welfare I had such great anguish; but Your love has delivered me from the pit of oblivion, for You have cast all my sins behind Your back.

verses.esv: For Sheol does not thank you; death does not praise you; those who go down to the pit do not hope for your faithfulness.
verses.kjv: For the grave cannot praise thee, death can not celebrate thee: they that go down into the pit cannot hope for thy truth.
verses.bsb: For Sheol cannot thank You; Death cannot praise You. Those who descend to the Pit cannot hope for Your faithfulness.

verses.esv: The living, the living, he thanks you, as I do this day; the father makes known to the children your faithfulness.
verses.kjv: The living, the living, he shall praise thee, as I do this day: the father to the children shall make known thy truth.
verses.bsb: The living, only the living, can thank You, as I do today; fathers will tell their children about Your faithfulness.

verses.esv: The LORD will save me, and we will play my music on stringed instruments all the days of our lives, at the house of the LORD.
verses.kjv: The Lord was ready to save me: therefore we will sing my songs to the stringed instruments all the days of our life in the house of the Lord.
verses.bsb: The LORD will save me; we will play songs on stringed instruments all the days of our lives in the house of the LORD.

verses.esv: Now Isaiah had said, “Let them take a cake of figs and apply it to the boil, that he may recover.”
verses.kjv: For Isaiah had said, Let them take a lump of figs, and lay it for a plaister upon the boil, and he shall recover.
verses.bsb: Now Isaiah had said, “Prepare a lump of pressed figs and apply it to the boil, and he will recover.”

verses.esv: Hezekiah also had said, “What is the sign that I shall go up to the house of the LORD?”
verses.kjv: Hezekiah also had said, What is the sign that I shall go up to the house of the Lord?
verses.bsb: And Hezekiah had asked, “What will be the sign that I will go up to the house of the LORD?”

verses.esv: At that time Merodach-baladan the son of Baladan, king of Babylon, sent envoys with letters and a present to Hezekiah, for he heard that he had been sick and had recovered.
verses.kjv: AT that time Merodach-baladan, the son of Baladan, king of Babylon, sent letters and a present to Hezekiah: for he had heard that he had been sick, and was recovered.
verses.bsb: At that time Merodach-baladan son of Baladan king of Babylon sent letters and a gift to Hezekiah, for he had heard about Hezekiah’s illness and recovery.

verses.esv: And Hezekiah welcomed them gladly. And he showed them his treasure house, the silver, the gold, the spices, the precious oil, his whole armory, all that was found in his storehouses. There was nothing in his house or in all his realm that Hezekiah did not show them.
verses.kjv: And Hezekiah was glad of them, and shewed them the house of his precious things, the silver, and the gold, and the spices, and the precious ointment, and all the house of his armour, and all that was found in his treasures: there was nothing in his house, nor in all his dominion, that Hezekiah shewed them not.
verses.bsb: And Hezekiah welcomed the envoys gladly and showed them what was in his treasure house—the silver, the gold, the spices, and the precious oil, as well as his entire armory—all that was found in his storehouses. There was nothing in his palace or in all his dominion that Hezekiah did not show them.

verses.esv: Then Isaiah the prophet came to King Hezekiah, and said to him, “What did these men say? And from where did they come to you?” Hezekiah said, “They have come to me from a far country, from Babylon.”
verses.kjv: ¶ Then came Isaiah the prophet unto king Hezekiah, and said unto him, What said these men? and from whence came they unto thee? And Hezekiah said, They are come from a far country unto me, even from Babylon.
verses.bsb: Then the prophet Isaiah went to King Hezekiah and asked, “Where did those men come from, and what did they say to you?”  “They came to me from a distant land,” Hezekiah replied, “from Babylon.”

verses.esv: He said, “What have they seen in your house?” Hezekiah answered, “They have seen all that is in my house. There is nothing in my storehouses that I did not show them.”
verses.kjv: Then said he, What have they seen in thine house? And Hezekiah answered, All that is in mine house have they seen: there is nothing among my treasures that I have not shewed them.
verses.bsb: “What have they seen in your palace?” Isaiah asked.  “They have seen everything in my palace,” answered Hezekiah. “There is nothing among my treasures that I did not show them.”

verses.esv: Then Isaiah said to Hezekiah, “Hear the word of the LORD of hosts:
verses.kjv: Then said Isaiah to Hezekiah, Hear the word of the Lord of hosts:
verses.bsb: Then Isaiah said to Hezekiah, “Hear the word of the LORD of Hosts:

verses.esv: Behold, the days are coming, when all that is in your house, and that which your fathers have stored up till this day, shall be carried to Babylon. Nothing shall be left, says the LORD.
verses.kjv: Behold, the days come, that all that is in thine house, and that which thy fathers have laid up in store until this day, shall be carried to Babylon: nothing shall be left, saith the Lord.
verses.bsb: The time will surely come when everything in your palace and all that your fathers have stored up until this day will be carried off to Babylon. Nothing will be left, says the LORD.

verses.esv: And some of your own sons, who will come from you, whom you will father, shall be taken away, and they shall be eunuchs in the palace of the king of Babylon.”
verses.kjv: And of thy sons that shall issue from thee, which thou shalt beget, shall they take away; and they shall be eunuchs in the palace of the king of Babylon.
verses.bsb: And some of your descendants, your own flesh and blood, will be taken away to be eunuchs in the palace of the king of Babylon.”

verses.esv: Then Hezekiah said to Isaiah, “The word of the LORD that you have spoken is good.” For he thought, “There will be peace and security in my days.”
verses.kjv: Then said Hezekiah to Isaiah, Good is the word of the Lord which thou hast spoken. He said moreover, For there shall be peace and truth in my days.
verses.bsb: But Hezekiah said to Isaiah, “The word of the LORD that you have spoken is good.” For he thought, “At least there will be peace and security in my lifetime.”

verses.esv: Comfort, comfort my people, says your God.
verses.kjv: COMFORT ye, comfort ye my people, saith your God.
verses.bsb: “Comfort, comfort My people,” says your God.

verses.esv: Speak tenderly to Jerusalem, and cry to her that her warfare is ended, that her iniquity is pardoned, that she has received from the LORD's hand double for all her sins.
verses.kjv: Speak ye comfortably to Jerusalem, and cry unto her, that her warfare is accomplished, that her iniquity is pardoned: for she hath received of the Lord’s hand double for all her sins.
verses.bsb: “Speak tenderly to Jerusalem, and proclaim to her that her forced labor has been completed; her iniquity has been pardoned. For she has received from the hand of the LORD double for all her sins.”

verses.esv: A voice cries: “In the wilderness prepare the way of the LORD; make straight in the desert a highway for our God.
verses.kjv: ¶ The voice of him that crieth in the wilderness, Prepare ye the way of the Lord, make straight in the desert a highway for our God.
verses.bsb: A voice of one calling:  “Prepare the way for the LORD in the wilderness; make a straight highway for our God in the desert.

verses.esv: Every valley shall be lifted up, and every mountain and hill be made low; the uneven ground shall become level, and the rough places a plain.
verses.kjv: Every valley shall be exalted, and every mountain and hill shall be made low: and the crooked shall be made straight, and the rough places plain:
verses.bsb: Every valley shall be lifted up, and every mountain and hill made low; the uneven ground will become smooth, and the rugged land a plain.

verses.esv: And the glory of the LORD shall be revealed, and all flesh shall see it together, for the mouth of the LORD has spoken.”
verses.kjv: And the glory of the Lord shall be revealed, and all flesh shall see it together: for the mouth of the Lord hath spoken it.
verses.bsb: And the glory of the LORD will be revealed, and all humanity together will see it. For the mouth of the LORD has spoken.”

verses.esv: A voice says, “Cry!” And I said, “What shall I cry?” All flesh is grass, and all its beauty is like the flower of the field.
verses.kjv: The voice said, Cry. And he said, What shall I cry? All flesh is grass, and all the goodliness thereof is as the flower of the field:
verses.bsb: A voice says, “Cry out!” And I asked, “What should I cry out?” “All flesh is like grass, and all its glory like the flowers of the field.

verses.esv: The grass withers, the flower fades when the breath of the LORD blows on it; surely the people are grass.
verses.kjv: The grass withereth, the flower fadeth: because the spirit of the Lord bloweth upon it: surely the people is grass.
verses.bsb: The grass withers and the flowers fall when the breath of the LORD blows on them; indeed, the people are grass.

verses.esv: The grass withers, the flower fades, but the word of our God will stand forever.
verses.kjv: The grass withereth, the flower fadeth: but the word of our God shall stand for ever.
verses.bsb: The grass withers and the flowers fall, but the word of our God stands forever.”

verses.esv: Go on up to a high mountain, O Zion, herald of good news; lift up your voice with strength, O Jerusalem, herald of good news; lift it up, fear not; say to the cities of Judah, “Behold your God!”
verses.kjv: ¶ O Zion, that bringest good tidings, get thee up into the high mountain; O Jerusalem, that bringest good tidings, lift up thy voice with strength; lift it up, be not afraid; say unto the cities of Judah, Behold your God!
verses.bsb: Go up on a high mountain, O Zion, herald of good news. Raise your voice loudly, O Jerusalem, herald of good news. Lift it up, do not be afraid! Say to the cities of Judah, “Here is your God!”

verses.esv: Behold, the Lord GOD comes with might, and his arm rules for him; behold, his reward is with him, and his recompense before him.
verses.kjv: Behold, the Lord God will come with strong hand, and his arm shall rule for him: behold, his reward is with him, and his work before him.
verses.bsb: Behold, the Lord GOD comes with might, and His arm establishes His rule. His reward is with Him, and His recompense accompanies Him.

verses.esv: He will tend his flock like a shepherd; he will gather the lambs in his arms; he will carry them in his bosom, and gently lead those that are with young.
verses.kjv: He shall feed his flock like a shepherd: he shall gather the lambs with his arm, and carry them in his bosom, and shall gently lead those that are with young.
verses.bsb: He tends His flock like a shepherd; He gathers the lambs in His arms and carries them close to His heart. He gently leads the nursing ewes.

verses.esv: Who has measured the waters in the hollow of his hand and marked off the heavens with a span, enclosed the dust of the earth in a measure and weighed the mountains in scales and the hills in a balance?
verses.kjv: ¶ Who hath measured the waters in the hollow of his hand, and meted out heaven with the span, and comprehended the dust of the earth in a measure, and weighed the mountains in scales, and the hills in a balance?
verses.bsb: Who has measured the waters in the hollow of his hand, or marked off the heavens with the span of his hand? Who has held the dust of the earth in a basket, or weighed the mountains on a scale and the hills with a balance?

verses.esv: Who has measured the Spirit of the LORD, or what man shows him his counsel?
verses.kjv: Who hath directed the Spirit of the Lord, or being his counseller hath taught him?
verses.bsb: Who has directed the Spirit of the LORD, or informed Him as His counselor?

verses.esv: Whom did he consult, and who made him understand? Who taught him the path of justice, and taught him knowledge, and showed him the way of understanding?
verses.kjv: With whom took he counsel, and who instructed him, and taught him in the path of judgment, and taught him knowledge, and shewed to him the way of understanding?
verses.bsb: Whom did He consult to enlighten Him, and who taught Him the paths of justice? Who imparted knowledge to Him and showed Him the way of understanding?

verses.esv: Behold, the nations are like a drop from a bucket, and are accounted as the dust on the scales; behold, he takes up the coastlands like fine dust.
verses.kjv: Behold, the nations are as a drop of a bucket, and are counted as the small dust of the balance: behold, he taketh up the isles as a very little thing.
verses.bsb: Surely the nations are like a drop in a bucket; they are considered a speck of dust on the scales; He lifts up the islands like fine dust.

verses.esv: Lebanon would not suffice for fuel, nor are its beasts enough for a burnt offering.
verses.kjv: And Lebanon is not sufficient to burn, nor the beasts thereof sufficient for a burnt offering.
verses.bsb: Lebanon is not sufficient for fuel, nor its animals enough for a burnt offering.

verses.esv: All the nations are as nothing before him, they are accounted by him as less than nothing and emptiness.
verses.kjv: All nations before him are as nothing; and they are counted to him less than nothing, and vanity.
verses.bsb: All the nations are as nothing before Him; He regards them as nothingness and emptiness.

verses.esv: To whom then will you liken God, or what likeness compare with him?
verses.kjv: ¶ To whom then will ye liken God? or what likeness will ye compare unto him?
verses.bsb: To whom will you liken God? To what image will you compare Him?

verses.esv: An idol! A craftsman casts it, and a goldsmith overlays it with gold and casts for it silver chains.
verses.kjv: The workman melteth a graven image, and the goldsmith spreadeth it over with gold, and casteth silver chains.
verses.bsb: To an idol that a craftsman casts and a metalworker overlays with gold and fits with silver chains?

verses.esv: He who is too impoverished for an offering chooses wood that will not rot; he seeks out a skillful craftsman to set up an idol that will not move.
verses.kjv: He that is so impoverished that he hath no oblation chooseth a tree that will not rot; he seeketh unto him a cunning workman to prepare a graven image, that shall not be moved.
verses.bsb: To one bereft of an offering who chooses wood that will not rot, who seeks a skilled craftsman to set up an idol that will not topple?

verses.esv: Do you not know? Do you not hear? Has it not been told you from the beginning? Have you not understood from the foundations of the earth?
verses.kjv: Have ye not known? have ye not heard? hath it not been told you from the beginning? have ye not understood from the foundations of the earth?
verses.bsb: Do you not know? Have you not heard? Has it not been declared to you from the beginning? Have you not understood since the foundation of the earth?

verses.esv: It is he who sits above the circle of the earth, and its inhabitants are like grasshoppers; who stretches out the heavens like a curtain, and spreads them like a tent to dwell in;
verses.kjv: It is he that sitteth upon the circle of the earth, and the inhabitants thereof are as grasshoppers; that stretcheth out the heavens as a curtain, and spreadeth them out as a tent to dwell in:
verses.bsb: He sits enthroned above the circle of the earth; its dwellers are like grasshoppers. He stretches out the heavens like a curtain, and spreads them out like a tent to dwell in.

verses.esv: who brings princes to nothing, and makes the rulers of the earth as emptiness.
verses.kjv: That bringeth the princes to nothing; he maketh the judges of the earth as vanity.
verses.bsb: He brings the princes to nothing and makes the rulers of the earth meaningless.

verses.esv: Scarcely are they planted, scarcely sown, scarcely has their stem taken root in the earth, when he blows on them, and they wither, and the tempest carries them off like stubble.
verses.kjv: Yea, they shall not be planted; yea, they shall not be sown: yea, their stock shall not take root in the earth: and he shall also blow upon them, and they shall wither, and the whirlwind shall take them away as stubble.
verses.bsb: No sooner are they planted, no sooner are they sown, no sooner have their stems taken root in the ground, than He blows on them and they wither, and a whirlwind sweeps them away like stubble.

verses.esv: To whom then will you compare me, that I should be like him? says the Holy One.
verses.kjv: To whom then will ye liken me, or shall I be equal? saith the Holy One.
verses.bsb: “To whom will you liken Me, or who is My equal?” asks the Holy One.

verses.esv: Lift up your eyes on high and see: who created these? He who brings out their host by number, calling them all by name; by the greatness of his might and because he is strong in power, not one is missing.
verses.kjv: Lift up your eyes on high, and behold who hath created these things, that bringeth out their host by number: he calleth them all by names by the greatness of his might, for that he is strong in power; not one faileth.
verses.bsb: Lift up your eyes on high: Who created all these? He leads forth the starry host by number; He calls each one by name. Because of His great power and mighty strength, not one of them is missing.

verses.esv: Why do you say, O Jacob, and speak, O Israel, “My way is hidden from the LORD, and my right is disregarded by my God”?
verses.kjv: Why sayest thou, O Jacob, and speakest, O Israel, My way is hid from the Lord, and my judgment is passed over from my God?
verses.bsb: Why do you say, O Jacob, and why do you assert, O Israel, “My way is hidden from the LORD, and my claim is ignored by my God”?

verses.esv: Have you not known? Have you not heard? The LORD is the everlasting God, the Creator of the ends of the earth. He does not faint or grow weary; his understanding is unsearchable.
verses.kjv: ¶ Hast thou not known? hast thou not heard, that the everlasting God, the Lord, the Creator of the ends of the earth, fainteth not, neither is weary? there is no searching of his understanding.
verses.bsb: Do you not know? Have you not heard? The LORD is the everlasting God, the Creator of the ends of the earth. He will not grow tired or weary; His understanding is beyond searching out.

verses.esv: He gives power to the faint, and to him who has no might he increases strength.
verses.kjv: He giveth power to the faint; and to them that have no might he increaseth strength.
verses.bsb: He gives power to the faint and increases the strength of the weak.

verses.esv: Even youths shall faint and be weary, and young men shall fall exhausted;
verses.kjv: Even the youths shall faint and be weary, and the young men shall utterly fall:
verses.bsb: Even youths grow tired and weary, and young men stumble and fall.

verses.esv: but they who wait for the LORD shall renew their strength; they shall mount up with wings like eagles; they shall run and not be weary; they shall walk and not faint.
verses.kjv: But they that wait upon the Lord shall renew their strength; they shall mount up with wings as eagles; they shall run, and not be weary; and they shall walk, and not faint.
verses.bsb: But those who wait upon the LORD will renew their strength; they will mount up with wings like eagles; they will run and not grow weary, they will walk and not faint.

verses.esv: Listen to me in silence, O coastlands; let the peoples renew their strength; let them approach, then let them speak; let us together draw near for judgment.
verses.kjv: KEEP silence before me, O islands; and let the people renew their strength: let them come near; then let them speak: let us come near together to judgment.
verses.bsb: “Be silent before Me, O islands, and let the peoples renew their strength. Let them come forward and testify; let us together draw near for judgment.

verses.esv: Who stirred up one from the east whom victory meets at every step? He gives up nations before him, so that he tramples kings underfoot; he makes them like dust with his sword, like driven stubble with his bow.
verses.kjv: Who raised up the righteous man from the east, called him to his foot, gave the nations before him, and made him rule over kings? he gave them as the dust to his sword, and as driven stubble to his bow.
verses.bsb: Who has aroused one from the east and called him to his feet in righteousness? He hands nations over to him and subdues kings before him. He turns them to dust with his sword, to windblown chaff with his bow.

verses.esv: He pursues them and passes on safely, by paths his feet have not trod.
verses.kjv: He pursued them, and passed safely; even by the way that he had not gone with his feet.
verses.bsb: He pursues them, going on safely, hardly touching the path with his feet.

verses.esv: Who has performed and done this, calling the generations from the beginning? I, the LORD, the first, and with the last; I am he.
verses.kjv: Who hath wrought and done it, calling the generations from the beginning? I the Lord, the first, and with the last; I am he.
verses.bsb: Who has performed this and carried it out, calling forth the generations from the beginning? I, the LORD—the first and the last— I am He.”

verses.esv: The coastlands have seen and are afraid; the ends of the earth tremble; they have drawn near and come.
verses.kjv: The isles saw it, and feared; the ends of the earth were afraid, drew near, and came.
verses.bsb: The islands see and fear; the ends of the earth tremble. They approach and come forward.

verses.esv: Everyone helps his neighbor and says to his brother, “Be strong!”
verses.kjv: They helped every one his neighbour; and every one said to his brother, Be of good courage.
verses.bsb: Each one helps the other and says to his brother, “Be strong!”

verses.esv: The craftsman strengthens the goldsmith, and he who smooths with the hammer him who strikes the anvil, saying of the soldering, “It is good”; and they strengthen it with nails so that it cannot be moved.
verses.kjv: So the carpenter encouraged the goldsmith, and he that smootheth with the hammer him that smote the anvil, saying, It is ready for the sodering: and he fastened it with nails, that it should not be moved.
verses.bsb: The craftsman encourages the goldsmith, and he who wields the hammer cheers him who strikes the anvil, saying of the welding, “It is good.” He nails it down so it will not be toppled.

verses.esv: But you, Israel, my servant, Jacob, whom I have chosen, the offspring of Abraham, my friend;
verses.kjv: But thou, Israel, art my servant, Jacob whom I have chosen, the seed of Abraham my friend.
verses.bsb: “But you, O Israel, My servant, Jacob, whom I have chosen, descendant of Abraham My friend—

verses.esv: you whom I took from the ends of the earth, and called from its farthest corners, saying to you, “You are my servant, I have chosen you and not cast you off”;
verses.kjv: Thou whom I have taken from the ends of the earth, and called thee from the chief men thereof, and said unto thee, Thou art my servant; I have chosen thee, and not cast thee away.
verses.bsb: I brought you from the ends of the earth and called you from its farthest corners. I said, ‘You are My servant.’ I have chosen and not rejected you.

verses.esv: fear not, for I am with you; be not dismayed, for I am your God; I will strengthen you, I will help you, I will uphold you with my righteous right hand.
verses.kjv: ¶ Fear thou not; for I am with thee: be not dismayed; for I am thy God: I will strengthen thee; yea, I will help thee; yea, I will uphold thee with the right hand of my righteousness.
verses.bsb: Do not fear, for I am with you; do not be afraid, for I am your God. I will strengthen you; I will surely help you; I will uphold you with My right hand of righteousness.

verses.esv: Behold, all who are incensed against you shall be put to shame and confounded; those who strive against you shall be as nothing and shall perish.
verses.kjv: Behold, all they that were incensed against thee shall be ashamed and confounded: they shall be as nothing; and they that strive with thee shall perish.
verses.bsb: Behold, all who rage against you will be ashamed and disgraced; those who contend with you will be reduced to nothing and will perish.

verses.esv: You shall seek those who contend with you, but you shall not find them; those who war against you shall be as nothing at all.
verses.kjv: Thou shalt seek them, and shalt not find them, even them that contended with thee: they that war against thee shall be as nothing, and as a thing of nought.
verses.bsb: You will seek them but will not find them. Those who wage war against you will come to nothing.

verses.esv: For I, the LORD your God, hold your right hand; it is I who say to you, “Fear not, I am the one who helps you.”
verses.kjv: For I the Lord thy God will hold thy right hand, saying unto thee, Fear not; I will help thee.
verses.bsb: For I am the LORD your God, who takes hold of your right hand and tells you: Do not fear, I will help you.

verses.esv: Fear not, you worm Jacob, you men of Israel! I am the one who helps you, declares the LORD; your Redeemer is the Holy One of Israel.
verses.kjv: Fear not, thou worm Jacob, and ye men of Israel; I will help thee, saith the Lord, and thy redeemer, the Holy One of Israel.
verses.bsb: Do not fear, O worm of Jacob, O few men of Israel. I will help you,” declares the LORD. “Your Redeemer is the Holy One of Israel.

verses.esv: Behold, I make of you a threshing sledge, new, sharp, and having teeth; you shall thresh the mountains and crush them, and you shall make the hills like chaff;
verses.kjv: Behold, I will make thee a new sharp threshing instrument having teeth: thou shalt thresh the mountains, and beat them small, and shalt make the hills as chaff.
verses.bsb: Behold, I will make you into a threshing sledge, new and sharp, with many teeth. You will thresh the mountains and crush them, and reduce the hills to chaff.

verses.esv: you shall winnow them, and the wind shall carry them away, and the tempest shall scatter them. And you shall rejoice in the LORD; in the Holy One of Israel you shall glory.
verses.kjv: Thou shalt fan them, and the wind shall carry them away, and the whirlwind shall scatter them: and thou shalt rejoice in the Lord, and shalt glory in the Holy One of Israel.
verses.bsb: You will winnow them, and a wind will carry them away; a gale will scatter them. But you will rejoice in the LORD; you will glory in the Holy One of Israel.

verses.esv: When the poor and needy seek water, and there is none, and their tongue is parched with thirst, I the LORD will answer them; I the God of Israel will not forsake them.
verses.kjv: When the poor and needy seek water, and there is none, and their tongue faileth for thirst, I the Lord will hear them, I the God of Israel will not forsake them.
verses.bsb: The poor and needy seek water, but there is none; their tongues are parched with thirst. I, the LORD, will answer them; I, the God of Israel, will not forsake them.

verses.esv: I will open rivers on the bare heights, and fountains in the midst of the valleys. I will make the wilderness a pool of water, and the dry land springs of water.
verses.kjv: I will open rivers in high places, and fountains in the midst of the valleys: I will make the wilderness a pool of water, and the dry land springs of water.
verses.bsb: I will open rivers on the barren heights, and fountains in the middle of the valleys. I will turn the desert into a pool of water, and the dry land into flowing springs.

verses.esv: I will put in the wilderness the cedar, the acacia, the myrtle, and the olive. I will set in the desert the cypress, the plane and the pine together,
verses.kjv: I will plant in the wilderness the cedar, the shittah tree, and the myrtle, and the oil tree; I will set in the desert the fir tree, and the pine, and the box tree together:
verses.bsb: I will plant cedars in the wilderness, acacias, myrtles, and olive trees. I will set cypresses in the desert, elms and boxwood together,

verses.esv: that they may see and know, may consider and understand together, that the hand of the LORD has done this, the Holy One of Israel has created it.
verses.kjv: That they may see, and know, and consider, and understand together, that the hand of the Lord hath done this, and the Holy One of Israel hath created it.
verses.bsb: so that all may see and know, may consider and understand, that the hand of the LORD has done this and the Holy One of Israel has created it.”

verses.esv: Set forth your case, says the LORD; bring your proofs, says the King of Jacob.
verses.kjv: Produce your cause, saith the Lord; bring forth your strong reasons, saith the King of Jacob.
verses.bsb: “Present your case,” says the LORD. “Submit your arguments,” says the King of Jacob.

verses.esv: Let them bring them, and tell us what is to happen. Tell us the former things, what they are, that we may consider them, that we may know their outcome; or declare to us the things to come.
verses.kjv: Let them bring them forth, and shew us what shall happen: let them shew the former things, what they be, that we may consider them, and know the latter end of them; or declare us things for to come.
verses.bsb: “Let them come and tell us what will happen. Tell the former things, so that we may reflect on them and know the outcome. Or announce to us what is coming.

verses.esv: Tell us what is to come hereafter, that we may know that you are gods; do good, or do harm, that we may be dismayed and terrified.
verses.kjv: Shew the things that are to come hereafter, that we may know that ye are gods: yea, do good, or do evil, that we may be dismayed, and behold it together.
verses.bsb: Tell us the things that are to come, so that we may know that you are gods. Yes, do something good or evil, that we may look on together in dismay.

verses.esv: Behold, you are nothing, and your work is less than nothing; an abomination is he who chooses you.
verses.kjv: Behold, ye are of nothing, and your work of nought: an abomination is he that chooseth you.
verses.bsb: Behold, you are nothing and your work is of no value. Anyone who chooses you is detestable.

verses.esv: I stirred up one from the north, and he has come, from the rising of the sun, and he shall call upon my name; he shall trample on rulers as on mortar, as the potter treads clay.
verses.kjv: I have raised up one from the north, and he shall come: from the rising of the sun shall he call upon my name: and he shall come upon princes as upon morter, and as the potter treadeth clay.
verses.bsb: I have raised up one from the north, and he has come— one from the east who calls on My name. He will march over rulers as if they were mortar, like a potter who treads the clay.

verses.esv: Who declared it from the beginning, that we might know, and beforehand, that we might say, “He is right”? There was none who declared it, none who proclaimed, none who heard your words.
verses.kjv: Who hath declared from the beginning, that we may know? and beforetime, that we may say, He is righteous? yea, there is none that sheweth, yea, there is none that declareth, yea, there is none that heareth your words.
verses.bsb: Who has declared this from the beginning, so that we may know, and from times past, so that we may say: ‘He was right’? No one announced it, no one foretold it, no one heard your words.

verses.esv: I was the first to say to Zion, “Behold, here they are!” and I give to Jerusalem a herald of good news.
verses.kjv: The first shall say to Zion, Behold, behold them: and I will give to Jerusalem one that bringeth good tidings.
verses.bsb: I was the first to tell Zion: ‘Look, here they are!’ And I gave to Jerusalem a herald of good news.

verses.esv: But when I look, there is no one; among these there is no counselor who, when I ask, gives an answer.
verses.kjv: For I beheld, and there was no man; even among them, and there was no counseller, that, when I asked of them, could answer a word.
verses.bsb: When I look, there is no one; there is no counselor among them; when I ask them, they have nothing to say.

verses.esv: Behold, they are all a delusion; their works are nothing; their metal images are empty wind.
verses.kjv: Behold, they are all vanity; their works are nothing: their molten images are wind and confusion.
verses.bsb: See, they are all a delusion; their works amount to nothing; their images are as empty as the wind.

verses.esv: Behold my servant, whom I uphold, my chosen, in whom my soul delights; I have put my Spirit upon him; he will bring forth justice to the nations.
verses.kjv: BEHOLD my servant, whom I uphold; mine elect, in whom my soul delighteth; I have put my spirit upon him: he shall bring forth judgment to the Gentiles.
verses.bsb: “Here is My Servant, whom I uphold, My Chosen One, in whom My soul delights. I will put My Spirit on Him, and He will bring justice to the nations.

verses.esv: He will not cry aloud or lift up his voice, or make it heard in the street;
verses.kjv: He shall not cry, nor lift up, nor cause his voice to be heard in the street.
verses.bsb: He will not cry out or raise His voice, nor make His voice heard in the streets.

verses.esv: a bruised reed he will not break, and a faintly burning wick he will not quench; he will faithfully bring forth justice.
verses.kjv: A bruised reed shall he not break, and the smoking flax shall he not quench: he shall bring forth judgment unto truth.
verses.bsb: A bruised reed He will not break and a smoldering wick He will not extinguish; He will faithfully bring forth justice.

verses.esv: He will not grow faint or be discouraged till he has established justice in the earth; and the coastlands wait for his law.
verses.kjv: He shall not fail nor be discouraged, till he have set judgment in the earth: and the isles shall wait for his law.
verses.bsb: He will not grow weak or discouraged before He has established justice on the earth. In His law the islands will put their hope.”

verses.esv: Thus says God, the LORD, who created the heavens and stretched them out, who spread out the earth and what comes from it, who gives breath to the people on it and spirit to those who walk in it:
verses.kjv: ¶ Thus saith God the Lord, he that created the heavens, and stretched them out; he that spread forth the earth, and that which cometh out of it; he that giveth breath unto the people upon it, and spirit to them that walk therein:
verses.bsb: This is what God the LORD says— He who created the heavens and stretched them out, who spread out the earth and its offspring, who gives breath to the people on it and life to those who walk in it:

verses.esv: “I am the LORD; I have called you in righteousness; I will take you by the hand and keep you; I will give you as a covenant for the people, a light for the nations,
verses.kjv: I the Lord have called thee in righteousness, and will hold thine hand, and will keep thee, and give thee for a covenant of the people, for a light of the Gentiles;
verses.bsb: “I, the LORD, have called you for a righteous purpose, and I will take hold of your hand. I will keep you and appoint you to be a covenant for the people and a light to the nations,

verses.esv: to open the eyes that are blind, to bring out the prisoners from the dungeon, from the prison those who sit in darkness.
verses.kjv: To open the blind eyes, to bring out the prisoners from the prison, and them that sit in darkness out of the prison house.
verses.bsb: to open the eyes of the blind, to bring prisoners out of the dungeon and those sitting in darkness out from the prison house.

verses.esv: I am the LORD; that is my name; my glory I give to no other, nor my praise to carved idols.
verses.kjv: I am the Lord: that is my name: and my glory will I not give to another, neither my praise to graven images.
verses.bsb: I am the LORD; that is My name! I will not yield My glory to another or My praise to idols.

verses.esv: Behold, the former things have come to pass, and new things I now declare; before they spring forth I tell you of them.”
verses.kjv: Behold, the former things are come to pass, and new things do I declare: before they spring forth I tell you of them.
verses.bsb: Behold, the former things have happened, and now I declare new things. Before they spring forth I proclaim them to you.”

verses.esv: Sing to the LORD a new song, his praise from the end of the earth, you who go down to the sea, and all that fills it, the coastlands and their inhabitants.
verses.kjv: Sing unto the Lord a new song, and his praise from the end of the earth, ye that go down to the sea, and all that is therein; the isles, and the inhabitants thereof.
verses.bsb: Sing to the LORD a new song— His praise from the ends of the earth— you who go down to the sea, and all that is in it, you islands, and all who dwell in them.

verses.esv: Let the desert and its cities lift up their voice, the villages that Kedar inhabits; let the habitants of Sela sing for joy, let them shout from the top of the mountains.
verses.kjv: Let the wilderness and the cities thereof lift up their voice, the villages that Kedar doth inhabit: let the inhabitants of the rock sing, let them shout from the top of the mountains.
verses.bsb: Let the desert and its cities raise their voices; let the villages of Kedar cry aloud. Let the people of Sela sing for joy; let them cry out from the mountaintops.

verses.esv: Let them give glory to the LORD, and declare his praise in the coastlands.
verses.kjv: Let them give glory unto the Lord, and declare his praise in the islands.
verses.bsb: Let them give glory to the LORD and declare His praise in the islands.

verses.esv: The LORD goes out like a mighty man, like a man of war he stirs up his zeal; he cries out, he shouts aloud, he shows himself mighty against his foes.
verses.kjv: The Lord shall go forth as a mighty man, he shall stir up jealousy like a man of war: he shall cry, yea, roar; he shall prevail against his enemies.
verses.bsb: The LORD goes forth like a mighty one; He stirs up His zeal like a warrior. He shouts; yes, He roars in triumph over His enemies:

verses.esv: For a long time I have held my peace; I have kept still and restrained myself; now I will cry out like a woman in labor; I will gasp and pant.
verses.kjv: I have long time holden my peace; I have been still, and refrained myself: now will I cry like a travailing woman; I will destroy and devour at once.
verses.bsb: “I have kept silent from ages past; I have remained quiet and restrained. But now I will groan like a woman in labor; I will at once gasp and pant.

verses.esv: I will lay waste mountains and hills, and dry up all their vegetation; I will turn the rivers into islands, and dry up the pools.
verses.kjv: I will make waste mountains and hills, and dry up all their herbs; and I will make the rivers islands, and I will dry up the pools.
verses.bsb: I will lay waste the mountains and hills and dry up all their vegetation. I will turn the rivers into dry land and drain the marshes.

verses.esv: And I will lead the blind in a way that they do not know, in paths that they have not known I will guide them. I will turn the darkness before them into light, the rough places into level ground. These are the things I do, and I do not forsake them.
verses.kjv: And I will bring the blind by a way that they knew not; I will lead them in paths that they have not known: I will make darkness light before them, and crooked things straight. These things will I do unto them, and not forsake them.
verses.bsb: I will lead the blind by a way they did not know; I will guide them on unfamiliar paths. I will turn darkness into light before them and rough places into level ground. These things I will do for them, and I will not forsake them.

verses.esv: They are turned back and utterly put to shame, who trust in carved idols, who say to metal images, “You are our gods.”
verses.kjv: ¶ They shall be turned back, they shall be greatly ashamed, that trust in graven images, that say to the molten images, Ye are our gods.
verses.bsb: But those who trust in idols and say to molten images, ‘You are our gods!’ will be turned back in utter shame.

verses.esv: Hear, you deaf, and look, you blind, that you may see!
verses.kjv: Hear, ye deaf; and look, ye blind, that ye may see.
verses.bsb: Listen, you deaf ones; look, you blind ones, that you may see!

verses.esv: Who is blind but my servant, or deaf as my messenger whom I send? Who is blind as my dedicated one, or blind as the servant of the LORD?
verses.kjv: Who is blind, but my servant? or deaf, as my messenger that I sent? who is blind as he that is perfect, and blind as the Lord’s servant?
verses.bsb: Who is blind but My servant, or deaf like the messenger I am sending? Who is blind like My covenant partner, or blind like the servant of the LORD?

verses.esv: He sees many things, but does not observe them; his ears are open, but he does not hear.
verses.kjv: Seeing many things, but thou observest not; opening the ears, but he heareth not.
verses.bsb: Though seeing many things, you do not keep watch. Though your ears are open, you do not hear.”

verses.esv: The LORD was pleased, for his righteousness' sake, to magnify his law and make it glorious.
verses.kjv: The Lord is well pleased for his righteousness’ sake; he will magnify the law, and make it honourable.
verses.bsb: The LORD was pleased, for the sake of His righteousness, to magnify His law and make it glorious.

verses.esv: But this is a people plundered and looted; they are all of them trapped in holes and hidden in prisons; they have become plunder with none to rescue, spoil with none to say, “Restore!”
verses.kjv: But this is a people robbed and spoiled; they are all of them snared in holes, and they are hid in prison houses: they are for a prey, and none delivereth; for a spoil, and none saith, Restore.
verses.bsb: But this is a people plundered and looted, all trapped in caves or imprisoned in dungeons. They have become plunder with no one to rescue them, and loot with no one to say, “Send them back!”

verses.esv: Who among you will give ear to this, will attend and listen for the time to come?
verses.kjv: Who among you will give ear to this? who will hearken and hear for the time to come?
verses.bsb: Who among you will pay attention to this? Who will listen and obey hereafter?

verses.esv: Who gave up Jacob to the looter, and Israel to the plunderers? Was it not the LORD, against whom we have sinned, in whose ways they would not walk, and whose law they would not obey?
verses.kjv: Who gave Jacob for a spoil, and Israel to the robbers? did not the Lord, he against whom we have sinned? for they would not walk in his ways, neither were they obedient unto his law.
verses.bsb: Who gave Jacob up for spoil, and Israel to the plunderers? Was it not the LORD, against whom we have sinned? They were unwilling to walk in His ways, and they would not obey His law.

verses.esv: So he poured on him the heat of his anger and the might of battle; it set him on fire all around, but he did not understand; it burned him up, but he did not take it to heart.
verses.kjv: Therefore he hath poured upon him the fury of his anger, and the strength of battle: and it hath set him on fire round about, yet he knew not; and it burned him, yet he laid it not to heart.
verses.bsb: So He poured out on them His furious anger and the fierceness of battle. It enveloped them in flames, but they did not understand; it consumed them, but they did not take it to heart.

verses.esv: But now thus says the LORD, he who created you, O Jacob, he who formed you, O Israel: “Fear not, for I have redeemed you; I have called you by name, you are mine.
verses.kjv: BUT now thus saith the Lord that created thee, O Jacob, and he that formed thee, O Israel, Fear not: for I have redeemed thee, I have called thee by thy name; thou art mine.
verses.bsb: Now this is what the LORD says— He who created you, O Jacob, and He who formed you, O Israel:   “Do not fear, for I have redeemed you; I have called you by your name; you are Mine!

verses.esv: When you pass through the waters, I will be with you; and through the rivers, they shall not overwhelm you; when you walk through fire you shall not be burned, and the flame shall not consume you.
verses.kjv: When thou passest through the waters, I will be with thee; and through the rivers, they shall not overflow thee: when thou walkest through the fire, thou shalt not be burned; neither shall the flame kindle upon thee.
verses.bsb: When you pass through the waters, I will be with you; and when you go through the rivers, they will not overwhelm you. When you walk through the fire, you will not be scorched; the flames will not set you ablaze.

verses.esv: For I am the LORD your God, the Holy One of Israel, your Savior. I give Egypt as your ransom, Cush and Seba in exchange for you.
verses.kjv: For I am the Lord thy God, the Holy One of Israel, thy Saviour: I gave Egypt for thy ransom, Ethiopia and Seba for thee.
verses.bsb: For I am the LORD your God, the Holy One of Israel, your Savior; I give Egypt for your ransom, Cush and Seba in your place.

verses.esv: Because you are precious in my eyes, and honored, and I love you, I give men in return for you, peoples in exchange for your life.
verses.kjv: Since thou wast precious in my sight, thou hast been honourable, and I have loved thee: therefore will I give men for thee, and people for thy life.
verses.bsb: Because you are precious and honored in My sight, and because I love you, I will give men in exchange for you and nations in place of your life.

verses.esv: Fear not, for I am with you; I will bring your offspring from the east, and from the west I will gather you.
verses.kjv: Fear not: for I am with thee: I will bring thy seed from the east, and gather thee from the west;
verses.bsb: Do not be afraid, for I am with you; I will bring your offspring from the east and gather you from the west.

verses.esv: I will say to the north, Give up, and to the south, Do not withhold; bring my sons from afar and my daughters from the end of the earth,
verses.kjv: I will say to the north, Give up; and to the south, Keep not back: bring my sons from far, and my daughters from the ends of the earth;
verses.bsb: I will say to the north, ‘Give them up!’ and to the south, ‘Do not hold them back!’ Bring My sons from afar, and My daughters from the ends of the earth—

verses.esv: everyone who is called by my name, whom I created for my glory, whom I formed and made.”
verses.kjv: Even every one that is called by my name: for I have created him for my glory, I have formed him; yea, I have made him.
verses.bsb: everyone called by My name and created for My glory, whom I have indeed formed and made.”

verses.esv: Bring out the people who are blind, yet have eyes, who are deaf, yet have ears!
verses.kjv: ¶ Bring forth the blind people that have eyes, and the deaf that have ears.
verses.bsb: Bring out a people who have eyes but are blind, and who have ears but are deaf.

verses.esv: All the nations gather together, and the peoples assemble. Who among them can declare this, and show us the former things? Let them bring their witnesses to prove them right, and let them hear and say, It is true.
verses.kjv: Let all the nations be gathered together, and let the people be assembled: who among them can declare this, and shew us former things? let them bring forth their witnesses, that they may be justified: or let them hear, and say, It is truth.
verses.bsb: All the nations gather together and the peoples assemble. Who among them can declare this, and proclaim to us the former things? Let them present their witnesses to vindicate them, so that others may hear and say, “It is true.”

verses.esv: “You are my witnesses,” declares the LORD, “and my servant whom I have chosen, that you may know and believe me and understand that I am he. Before me no god was formed, nor shall there be any after me.
verses.kjv: Ye are my witnesses, saith the Lord, and my servant whom I have chosen: that ye may know and believe me, and understand that I am he: before me there was no God formed, neither shall there be after me.
verses.bsb: “You are My witnesses,” declares the LORD, “and My servant whom I have chosen, so that you may consider and believe Me and understand that I am He. Before Me no god was formed, and after Me none will come.

verses.esv: I, I am the LORD, and besides me there is no savior.
verses.kjv: I, even I, am the Lord; and beside me there is no saviour.
verses.bsb: I, yes I, am the LORD, and there is no Savior but Me.

verses.esv: I declared and saved and proclaimed, when there was no strange god among you; and you are my witnesses,” declares the LORD, “and I am God.
verses.kjv: I have declared, and have saved, and I have shewed, when there was no strange god among you: therefore ye are my witnesses, saith the Lord, that I am God.
verses.bsb: I alone decreed and saved and proclaimed— I, and not some foreign god among you. So you are My witnesses,” declares the LORD, “that I am God.

verses.esv: Also henceforth I am he; there is none who can deliver from my hand; I work, and who can turn it back?”
verses.kjv: Yea, before the day was I am he; and there is none that can deliver out of my hand: I will work, and who shall let it?
verses.bsb: Even from eternity I am He, and none can deliver out of My hand. When I act, who can reverse it?”

verses.esv: Thus says the LORD, your Redeemer, the Holy One of Israel: “For your sake I send to Babylon and bring them all down as fugitives, even the Chaldeans, in the ships in which they rejoice.
verses.kjv: ¶ Thus saith the Lord, your redeemer, the Holy One of Israel; For your sake I have sent to Babylon, and have brought down all their nobles, and the Chaldeans, whose cry is in the ships.
verses.bsb: Thus says the LORD your Redeemer, the Holy One of Israel:   “For your sake, I will send to Babylon and bring them all as fugitives, even the Chaldeans, in the ships in which they rejoice.

verses.esv: I am the LORD, your Holy One, the Creator of Israel, your King.”
verses.kjv: I am the Lord, your Holy One, the creator of Israel, your King.
verses.bsb: I am the LORD, your Holy One, the Creator of Israel, and your King.”

verses.esv: Thus says the LORD, who makes a way in the sea, a path in the mighty waters,
verses.kjv: Thus saith the Lord, which maketh a way in the sea, and a path in the mighty waters;
verses.bsb: Thus says the LORD, who makes a way in the sea and a path through the surging waters,

verses.esv: who brings forth chariot and horse, army and warrior; they lie down, they cannot rise, they are extinguished, quenched like a wick:
verses.kjv: Which bringeth forth the chariot and horse, the army and the power; they shall lie down together, they shall not rise: they are extinct, they are quenched as tow.
verses.bsb: who brings out the chariots and horses, the armies and warriors together, to lie down, never to rise again; to be extinguished, snuffed out like a wick:

verses.esv: “Remember not the former things, nor consider the things of old.
verses.kjv: ¶ Remember ye not the former things, neither consider the things of old.
verses.bsb: “Do not call to mind the former things; pay no attention to the things of old.

verses.esv: Behold, I am doing a new thing; now it springs forth, do you not perceive it? I will make a way in the wilderness and rivers in the desert.
verses.kjv: Behold, I will do a new thing; now it shall spring forth; shall ye not know it? I will even make a way in the wilderness, and rivers in the desert.
verses.bsb: Behold, I am about to do something new; even now it is coming. Do you not see it? Indeed, I will make a way in the wilderness and streams in the desert.

verses.esv: The wild beasts will honor me, the jackals and the ostriches, for I give water in the wilderness, rivers in the desert, to give drink to my chosen people,
verses.kjv: The beast of the field shall honour me, the dragons and the owls: because I give waters in the wilderness, and rivers in the desert, to give drink to my people, my chosen.
verses.bsb: The beasts of the field will honor Me, the jackals and the ostriches, because I provide water in the wilderness and rivers in the desert, to give drink to My chosen people.

verses.esv: the people whom I formed for myself that they might declare my praise.
verses.kjv: This people have I formed for myself; they shall shew forth my praise.
verses.bsb: The people I formed for Myself will declare My praise.

verses.esv: “Yet you did not call upon me, O Jacob; but you have been weary of me, O Israel!
verses.kjv: ¶ But thou hast not called upon me, O Jacob; but thou hast been weary of me, O Israel.
verses.bsb: But you have not called on Me, O Jacob, because you have grown weary of Me, O Israel.

verses.esv: You have not brought me your sheep for burnt offerings, or honored me with your sacrifices. I have not burdened you with offerings, or wearied you with frankincense.
verses.kjv: Thou hast not brought me the small cattle of thy burnt offerings; neither hast thou honoured me with thy sacrifices. I have not caused thee to serve with an offering, nor wearied thee with incense.
verses.bsb: You have not brought Me sheep for burnt offerings, nor honored Me with your sacrifices. I have not burdened you with offerings, nor wearied you with frankincense.

verses.esv: You have not bought me sweet cane with money, or satisfied me with the fat of your sacrifices. But you have burdened me with your sins; you have wearied me with your iniquities.
verses.kjv: Thou hast bought me no sweet cane with money, neither hast thou filled me with the fat of thy sacrifices: but thou hast made me to serve with thy sins, thou hast wearied me with thine iniquities.
verses.bsb: You have not bought Me sweet cane with your silver, nor satisfied Me with the fat of your sacrifices. But you have burdened Me with your sins; you have wearied Me with your iniquities.

verses.esv: “I, I am he who blots out your transgressions for my own sake, and I will not remember your sins.
verses.kjv: I, even I, am he that blotteth out thy transgressions for mine own sake, and will not remember thy sins.
verses.bsb: I, yes I, am He who blots out your transgressions for My own sake and remembers your sins no more.

verses.esv: Put me in remembrance; let us argue together; set forth your case, that you may be proved right.
verses.kjv: Put me in remembrance: let us plead together: declare thou, that thou mayest be justified.
verses.bsb: Remind Me, let us argue the matter together. State your case, so that you may be vindicated.

verses.esv: Your first father sinned, and your mediators transgressed against me.
verses.kjv: Thy first father hath sinned, and thy teachers have transgressed against me.
verses.bsb: Your first father sinned, and your spokesmen rebelled against Me.

verses.esv: Therefore I will profane the princes of the sanctuary, and deliver Jacob to utter destruction and Israel to reviling.
verses.kjv: Therefore I have profaned the princes of the sanctuary, and have given Jacob to the curse, and Israel to reproaches.
verses.bsb: So I will disgrace the princes of your sanctuary, and I will devote Jacob to destruction and Israel to reproach.”

verses.esv: “But now hear, O Jacob my servant, Israel whom I have chosen!
verses.kjv: YET now hear, O Jacob my servant; and Israel, whom I have chosen:
verses.bsb: But now listen, O Jacob My servant, Israel, whom I have chosen.

verses.esv: Thus says the LORD who made you, who formed you from the womb and will help you: Fear not, O Jacob my servant, Jeshurun whom I have chosen.
verses.kjv: Thus saith the Lord that made thee, and formed thee from the womb, which will help thee; Fear not, O Jacob, my servant; and thou, Jesurun, whom I have chosen.
verses.bsb: This is the word of the LORD, your Maker, who formed you from the womb and who will help you: “Do not be afraid, O Jacob My servant, Jeshurun, whom I have chosen.

verses.esv: For I will pour water on the thirsty land, and streams on the dry ground; I will pour my Spirit upon your offspring, and my blessing on your descendants.
verses.kjv: For I will pour water upon him that is thirsty, and floods upon the dry ground: I will pour my spirit upon thy seed, and my blessing upon thine offspring:
verses.bsb: For I will pour water on the thirsty land, and currents on the dry ground. I will pour out My Spirit on your descendants, and My blessing on your offspring.

verses.esv: They shall spring up among the grass like willows by flowing streams.
verses.kjv: And they shall spring up as among the grass, as willows by the water courses.
verses.bsb: They will sprout among the grass like willows by flowing streams.

verses.esv: This one will say, ‘I am the LORD's,’ another will call on the name of Jacob, and another will write on his hand, ‘The LORD's,’ and name himself by the name of Israel.”
verses.kjv: One shall say, I am the Lord’s; and another shall call himself by the name of Jacob; and another shall subscribe with his hand unto the Lord, and surname himself by the name of Israel.
verses.bsb: One will say, ‘I belong to the LORD,’ another will call himself by the name of Jacob, and still another will write on his hand, ‘The LORD’s,’ and will take the name of Israel.”

verses.esv: Thus says the LORD, the King of Israel and his Redeemer, the LORD of hosts: “I am the first and I am the last; besides me there is no god.
verses.kjv: Thus saith the Lord the King of Israel, and his redeemer the Lord of hosts; I am the first, and I am the last; and beside me there is no God.
verses.bsb: Thus says the LORD, the King and Redeemer of Israel, the LORD of Hosts:   “I am the first and I am the last, and there is no God but Me.

verses.esv: Who is like me? Let him proclaim it. Let him declare and set it before me, since I appointed an ancient people. Let them declare what is to come, and what will happen.
verses.kjv: And who, as I, shall call, and shall declare it, and set it in order for me, since I appointed the ancient people? and the things that are coming, and shall come, let them shew unto them.
verses.bsb: Who then is like Me? Let him say so! Let him declare his case before Me, since I established an ancient people. Let him foretell the things to come, and what is to take place.

verses.esv: Fear not, nor be afraid; have I not told you from of old and declared it? And you are my witnesses! Is there a God besides me? There is no Rock; I know not any.”
verses.kjv: Fear ye not, neither be afraid: have not I told thee from that time, and have declared it? ye are even my witnesses. Is there a God beside me? yea, there is no God; I know not any.
verses.bsb: Do not tremble or fear. Have I not told you and declared it long ago? You are My witnesses! Is there any God but Me? There is no other Rock; I know not one.”

verses.esv: All who fashion idols are nothing, and the things they delight in do not profit. Their witnesses neither see nor know, that they may be put to shame.
